html:
[209]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 指其女阿娇好否 - Colloquial expression asking about the appearance or quality of someone, specifically about the beauty of the girl named Aqiao.
  • 若得阿娇作妇，当作金屋贮之 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'If I could get Aqiao as my wife, I would build a golden house for her.' Alludes to the famous anecdote of Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 苦要上 - Colloquial expression meaning 'persistently pressuring the emperor.'
  • 遂成婚焉 - Formal expression meaning 'and thus they were married.'
  • 皇后既废 - Formal expression meaning 'the empress was demoted.'
  • 栗姬次，应立 - Formal expression meaning 'Liji was next in line to be made empress.'
  • 长主伺其短，辄微白之 - Formal expression meaning 'the Empress Dowager watched for her faults and secretly reported them to the emperor.'
  • 属诸姬子曰 - Formal expression meaning 'entrusted the other concubines' sons with the message.'
  • 吾百岁后善视之 - Formal expression meaning 'After I am gone, treat them well.'
  • 栗姬怒，弗肯应 - Colloquial expression meaning 'Liji became angry and did not respond.'
  • 又骂上老狗 - Colloquial expression meaning 'she also called the emperor an old dog.'
  • 上心衔之未发也 - Formal expression meaning 'The emperor harbored resentment but did not act on it.'
  • 日谮之 - Formal expression meaning 'persistently slandered her.'
  • 因誉王夫人男之美 - Formal expression meaning 'and praised the beauty of Wang's son.'
  • 王夫人阴告长主 - Formal expression meaning 'Wang secretly informed the Empress Dowager.'
  • 使大臣请立栗姬为后 - Formal expression meaning 'to have the ministers petition for Liji to be made empress.'
  • 上以为栗姬讽之 - Formal expression meaning 'The emperor thought Liji was mocking him.'
  • 遂发怒，诛大臣，废太子为王 - Formal expression meaning 'He became furious, executed the ministers, and demoted the crown prince to a lesser rank.'
  • 栗姬自杀 - Formal expression meaning 'Liji committed suicide.'
  • 遂立王夫人为后，胶东王为太子 - Formal expression meaning 'Wang was made empress, and the Prince of Jiaodong was made crown prince.'
  • 时年七岁 - Formal expression meaning 'at the age of seven.'
  • 彘者彻也 - Formal expression meaning 'The character '彘' means 'thorough.''
  • 因改名彻 - Formal expression meaning 'Therefore, he changed his name to Che.'
  • 廷尉上囚防年 - Formal expression meaning 'The Grand Judge presented the prisoner Fang Nian.'
  • 继母陈氏杀年父 - Formal expression meaning 'Fang's stepmother Chen killed his father.'
  • 年因杀陈 - Formal expression meaning 'Fang then killed Chen.'
  • 依律，杀母大逆论 - Formal expression meaning 'According to the law, killing one's mother is considered a grave offense.'
  • 帝疑之 - Formal expression meaning 'The emperor doubted this.'
  • 诏问太子 - Formal expression meaning 'issued an edict to consult the crown prince.'
  • 对曰 - Formal expression meaning 'replied.'
  • 夫继母如母，明其不及也 - Formal expression meaning 'A stepmother is like a mother, but it is clear that she is not as close.'
  • 缘父之爱，故谓之母尔 - Formal expression meaning 'Because of the father's affection, she is called a mother.'
  • 今继母无状，手杀其父 - Formal expression meaning 'Now, if a stepmother kills her husband, it is clear that the mother-child relationship is severed.'
  • 下手之日，母恩绝矣 - Formal expression meaning 'From the moment she kills him, the mother's love is severed.'
  • 宜与杀人者同，不宜大逆论 - Formal expression meaning 'It should be treated the same as murder, not as a grave offense.'
  • 帝从之，议者称善 - Formal expression meaning 'The emperor agreed, and those who debated the matter praised it.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 阿娇 - The name of a famous concubine of Emperor Wu of Han, known for the 'Golden House' story.
  • 长主 - Refers to Empress Dowager Dou, the mother of Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 栗姬 - A concubine of Emperor Wu of Han, who was demoted and eventually committed suicide.
  • 王夫人 - Another concubine of Emperor Wu of Han, who was later made empress.
  • 胶东王 - The Prince of Jiaodong, who was the son of Wang and later became the crown prince.
  • 彘者彻也 - The character '彘' (pig) is often associated with the character '彻' (thorough), which is a play on words in Chinese.
  • 廷尉 - The highest judicial official in the Han dynasty.
  • 防年 - A historical figure involved in a legal case.

Proper Nouns

  • 阿娇 - The name of a concubine of Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 长主 - Empress Dowager Dou, the mother of Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 栗姬 - A concubine of Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 王夫人 - Another concubine of Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 胶东王 - The Prince of Jiaodong, son of Wang.
  • 防年 - A historical figure involved in a legal case.
  • 陈氏 - The stepmother of Fang.

[210]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 改号建元 - Changed the era name to Jianyuan.
  • 长主伐其功 - The elder princess boasted of her merits.
  • 求欲无厌 - Her demands were insatiable.
  • 上患之 - The emperor was troubled by this.
  • 皇后宠亦衰 - The empress's favor also waned.
  • 汝新即位 - You have just ascended the throne.
  • 先为明堂 - First, build the Hall of Brightness.
  • 太皇太后己怒 - The Grand Empress Dowager was already angry.
  • 今又忤长主 - Now, you are also at odds with the elder princess.
  • 必重得罪 - You will surely incur severe blame.
  • 妇人性易悦 - Women's nature is easily pleased.
  • 深慎之 - Be deeply cautious.
  • 上纳太后戒 - The emperor heeded the Empress Dowager's advice.
  • 复与长主和 - He reconciled with the elder princess.
  • 皇后宠幸如初 - The empress regained her favor as before.
  • 建元六年 - In the sixth year of Jianyuan.
  • 太皇太后崩 - The Grand Empress Dowager passed away.
  • 上始亲政事 - The emperor began to personally govern.
  • 好祀鬼神 - He liked to worship ghosts and deities.
  • 谋议征伐 - He planned and discussed military campaigns.
  • 长主自伐滋甚 - The elder princess boasted even more.
  • 每有所求，上不复与 - Whenever she made demands, the emperor no longer granted them.
  • 长主怨望 - The elder princess harbored resentment.
  • 愈出丑言 - She spoke even more offensive words.
  • 上怒 - The emperor was angry.
  • 欲废 - Wanted to depose her.
  • 微长公主弗及此 - Without the elder princess, I would not have reached this position.
  • 忘德弗祥 - To forget her virtue would be inauspicious.
  • 且容之 - Let us tolerate her.
  • 乃止 - And so he stopped.
  • 然皇后宠虽衰，娇妒滋甚 - However, the empress's favor, though waning, became even more arrogant and jealous.
  • 女巫楚服 - The female witch named Chufu.
  • 自言有术能令上意回 - She claimed to have a method to change the emperor's mind.
  • 昼夜祭祀，合药服之 - She performed day and night sacrifices and took the concocted medicine.
  • 巫著男子衣冠帻带 - The witch dressed in men's attire, including hats and belts.
  • 素与皇后寝居，相爱若夫妇 - She usually slept and lived with the empress, loving each other like husband and wife.
  • 上闻，穷治侍御 - When the emperor heard of this, he thoroughly investigated the attendants.
  • 巫与后诸妖蛊咒咀 - The witch and the empress engaged in various spells and curses.
  • 女而男淫 - Engaging in sexual relations with men.
  • 皆伏辜 - All were found guilty.
  • 废皇后处长门宫 - The empress was deposed and placed in the Longmen Palace.
  • 后虽废，供养如法 - Although deposed, she was still treated according to the rules.
  • 长门无异其宫也 - The Longmen Palace was no different from her previous palace.
  • 长主以宿恩犹自亲近 - The elder princess, due to her long-standing favor, still maintained close relations.
  • 后置酒主家 - Later, she held a banquet at the elder princess's home.
  • 见所幸董偃 - She saw her favorite, Dong Yan.
  • 上为之起 - The emperor stood up for her.
  • 偃能自媚于上 - Dong Yan was able to flatter the emperor.
  • 贵宠闻于天下 - His favor and prestige were known throughout the land.
  • 尝宴饮宣室 - Once, he held a banquet in the Xuan Room.
  • 引公主及偃 - He invited the elder princess and Dong Yan.

Proper Nouns

  • 太皇太后 - Grand Empress Dowager.
  • 长主 - Elder princess.
  • 皇后 - Empress.
  • 董偃 - Name of the favorite.
  • 宣室 - Name of the room.

[211]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 主怒 - Means 'the emperor's anger.'
  • 闭于内 - Means 'confined inside' or 'locked up.'
  • 不复听交游 - Means 'no longer allowed to socialize' or 'forbidden from interacting with others.'
  • 赐偃死 - Means 'bestowed death' or 'ordered his execution.'
  • 遂得幸 - Means 'was eventually favored' or 'became favored.'
  • 上见其美发悦之 - Means 'the emperor saw her beautiful hair and was pleased by it.'
  • 纳于宫中 - Means 'taken into the palace.'
  • 择宫人不中用者出之 - Means 'selected and expelled those palace women who were not useful.'
  • 泣涕请出 - Means 'cried and pleaded to be released.'
  • 吾昨夜梦子夫 - Means 'I had a dream about Zifū last night.'
  • 中庭生梓树数株 - Means 'several paulownia trees grew in the courtyard.'
  • 岂非天意乎 - Means 'isn't this the will of heaven?'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • Refers to the emperor's anger, which was a significant political event.
  • Indicates the practice of isolating someone, often a political prisoner or a disgraced person.
  • Indicates the emperor's authority to order executions.
  • Refers to the process of becoming favored by the emperor, often through sexual relations.
  • Highlights the importance of physical beauty in attracting the emperor's attention.
  • Refers to the practice of taking women into the imperial harem.
  • Indicates the management of the imperial harem, where women who were not favored were often expelled.
  • Shows the desperation and emotional state of the women in the harem.
  • Refers to the belief in dreams as omens or signs of divine will.
  • Symbolizes the emperor's favor and the idea that the dream was a sign of divine approval.
  • Emphasizes the belief in the will of heaven and the divine right of the emperor.

Proper Nouns

  • 主 - The emperor.
  • 偃 - The person who was executed.
  • 平阳公主 - Princess Pingyang.
  • 子夫 - Zifū, the woman who became favored by the emperor.
  • 上 - The emperor.
  • 宫 - The palace.
  • 宫女 - Palace women.
  • 元朔元年 - The first year of the Yuanshuo era, a period in the Han dynasty.

[212]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 凡三幸生二女，后生男，即戾太子也 - Altogether had three pregnancies, resulting in two daughters and then a son, who is identified as the Prince of Li.
  • 淮南王安招方术之上，皆谓神仙 - Attracted those skilled in alchemy, all of whom were considered able to achieve immortality.
  • 上闻而喜女事 - Took pleasure in or was interested in female-related matters.
  • 少翁云能致其神 - Claimed to be able to summon her spirit.
  • 乃夜张帐，明烛陈酒食 - Set up a tent, lit candles, and arranged wine and food.
  • 遥见李夫人，不得就视也 - Saw her from a distance but could not approach to see her.
  • 惨郁郁其芜秽兮，处幽隐而怀伤 - Describes a state of gloominess, decay, and living in seclusion while harboring grief.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 戾太子 - Refers to Prince Li, the son of Emperor Wu of Han, involved in the 'Princes' Rebellion' and accused of treason.
  • 淮南王安 - Refers to Liu An, the King of Huainan, known for his interest in alchemy and immortality.
  • 李少翁 - A famous alchemist and magician during the reign of Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 李夫人 - A concubine of Emperor Wu of Han, known for her beauty and the emperor's deep affection for her.

[213]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 释舆马于山椒兮，奄修夜之不阳 - Poetic and ambiguous expression; suggests releasing horses or carriages at the mountain peak and a long night without sunlight.
  • 云少翁者诸方皆验，惟祭太乙积年元应 - All methods were tested, but only the ritual of sacrificing to Taiyi after many years had the desired effect.
  • 为吾谢上，不能忍少日而败大事乎 - Convey my message to the emperor; cannot you endure a few days and ruin the great matter?
  • 上好自爱，后四十年求我于蓬山 - The emperor should take good care of himself; in forty years, seek me on Penglai Mountain.
  • 方将共事，不相怨也 - We will work together; we will not blame each other.
  • 于是，上大悔，复征诸方士 - Therefore, the emperor regretted and again summoned various alchemists.
  • 上常轻服为微行 - The emperor often dressed lightly to travel incognito.
  • 时丞相公孙弘数谏弗从 - At that time, Chancellor Gong Sun Hong often advised but was not heeded.
  • 弘谓其子曰：“吾年已八十余，陛下擢为宰相，士犹为知己死，况不世之君乎” - Gong Sun Hong told his son: I am over eighty years old, the emperor has appointed me as chancellor, a man would still die for a friend, how much more so for an extraordinary ruler?
  • 今陛下微行不已，社稷必危 - Now the emperor continues to travel incognito, the state will be in danger.
  • 吾虽不逮史鱼，冀万一能以尸谏 - I may not be as virtuous as Shi Yu, but I hope that perhaps I can die to advise the emperor.
  • 因自杀 - Therefore committed suicide.
  • 上闻而悲之，自为诔 - The emperor heard and was saddened, wrote a eulogy himself.
  • 弘尝谏伐匈奴 - Gong Sun Hong once advised against attacking the Xiongnu.
  • 为之少止 - It was somewhat reduced.
  • 弘卒，乃大发卒数十万，遣霍去病讨匈奴 - Gong Sun Hong died, then mobilized tens of thousands of soldiers and sent Huo Qubing to attack the Xiongnu.
  • 折兰过居延，获祭天金人于上林凿昆明池，又起柏梁台，以处神君 - Crossed the Shalang and Juyan, captured a golden man used for worship in the Upper Forest and dug the Kunming Pool, also built the Bailing Terrace to house the Divine Lady.
  • 神君者，长陵女子也 - The Divine Lady was a woman from Changling.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 少翁 - Famous alchemist and magician during the reign of Emperor Wen of Han.
  • 太乙 - Deity in Chinese mythology, often associated with the supreme ruler of the heavens.
  • 文成 - Title or name, possibly referring to a specific individual.
  • 关东 - Refers to the eastern regions of China, particularly the area east of the Hangu Pass.
  • 渭亭 - Place name, likely a small town or station along the Wei River.
  • 蓬山 - Penglai Mountain, a mythical island in Chinese folklore known for its immortals.
  • 公孙弘 - Prominent statesman and chancellor during the reign of Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 史鱼 - Historical figure known for his integrity and willingness to sacrifice himself for the sake of his principles.
  • 匈奴 - The Xiongnu, a nomadic confederation that frequently clashed with the Han dynasty.
  • 霍去病 - Renowned general of the Han dynasty, famous for his military campaigns against the Xiongnu.
  • 上林 - Imperial hunting park and gardens.
  • 昆明池 - Large artificial lake in Chang'an (modern Xi'an), used for training naval forces.
  • 柏梁台 - Famous palace in Chang'an, known for its luxurious architecture.
  • 神君 - Divine Lady, a deity or spirit revered in the Han dynasty.
  • 长陵 - Mausoleum of Emperor Gaozu of Han, located near modern Xi'an.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 释舆马于山椒兮，奄修夜之不阳 - Poetic and somewhat ambiguous; suggests releasing horses or carriages at the mountain peak and a long night without sunlight.
  • 云少翁者诸方皆验，惟祭太乙积年元应 - Elliptical clause; all methods were tested, but only the ritual of sacrificing to Taiyi after many years had the desired effect.
  • 上怒，诛之 - Concise sentence; the emperor was angry and executed him.
  • 文成被诛，后月余使者籍资从关东还，逢于渭亭 - Complex sequence of events; the messenger was executed, returned from the east, and met at Weiting.
  • 为吾谢上，不能忍少日而败大事乎 - Direct address; convey my message to the emperor; cannot you endure a few days and ruin the great matter?
  • 上好自爱，后四十年求我于蓬山 - Conditional statement; the emperor should take good care of himself; in forty years, seek me on Penglai Mountain.
  • 方将共事，不相怨也 - Promise; we will work together; we will not blame each other.
  • 于是，上大悔，复征诸方士 - Reaction; therefore, the emperor regretted and again summoned various alchemists.
  • 上常轻服为微行 - Description; the emperor often dressed lightly to travel incognito.
  • 时丞相公孙弘数谏弗从 - Statement of repeated advice; at that time, Chancellor Gong Sun Hong often advised but was not heeded.
  • 弘谓其子曰：“吾年已八十余，陛下擢为宰相，士犹为知己死，况不世之君乎” - Gong Sun Hong told his son: I am over eighty years old, the emperor has appointed me as chancellor, a man would still die for a friend, how much more so for an extraordinary ruler?
  • 今陛下微行不已，社稷必危 - Now the emperor continues to travel incognito, the state will be in danger.
  • 吾虽不逮史鱼，冀万一能以尸谏 - I may not be as virtuous as Shi Yu, but I hope that perhaps I can die to advise the emperor.
  • 因自杀 - Therefore committed suicide.
  • 上闻而悲之，自为诔 - The emperor heard and was saddened, wrote a eulogy himself.
  • 弘尝谏伐匈奴 - Gong Sun Hong once advised against attacking the Xiongnu.
  • 为之少止 - It was somewhat reduced.
  • 弘卒，乃大发卒数十万，遣霍去病讨匈奴 - Gong Sun Hong died, then mobilized tens of thousands of soldiers and sent Huo Qubing to attack the Xiongnu.
  • 折兰过居延，获祭天金人于上林凿昆明池，又起柏梁台，以处神君 - Crossed the Shalang and Juyan, captured a golden man used for worship in the Upper Forest and dug the Kunming Pool, also built the Bailing Terrace to house the Divine Lady.
  • 神君者，长陵女子也 - The Divine Lady was a woman from Changling.

[214]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 先嫁为人妻 - Refers to a woman who was married for the first time, a common practice in ancient Chinese society.
  • 生一男数岁死 - Indicates the death of a child, a common tragedy in ancient times.
  • 女子悲哀悼痛之亦死 - Suggests the cultural practice of mourning and the impact of grief on health.
  • 死而有灵 - Refers to the belief in spirits and the afterlife, a common belief in ancient China.
  • 祀之 - Indicates the practice of ancestor worship, a significant aspect of Chinese culture.
  • 说人家小事颇有验 - Suggests the belief in the power of spirits to predict future events.
  • 上遂祠神君请术 - Indicates the emperor's interest in spiritual and supernatural knowledge.
  • 数自祷于神君 - Indicates the practice of praying to spirits for guidance and protection.
  • 神君乃见其形 - Suggests the belief in the manifestation of spirits.
  • 自修饰 - Indicates the cultural practice of dressing up for special occasions.
  • 欲与去病交接 - Suggests the cultural and moral implications of intimate relationships.
  • 去病不肯 - Indicates Huo Qubing's moral integrity and rejection of inappropriate advances.
  • 乃责之曰 - Indicates the cultural practice of rebuking and moral censure.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 霍去病 - Refers to Huo Qubing, a famous Han dynasty general known for his military prowess.

[215]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 朔呼短人曰 - Direct quote where Sima Qian asks a dwarf if the mother of the giant has returned.
  • 巨灵阿母还来否 - Asks if the mother of the giant has returned.
  • 王母种桃，三千年一结子 - Refers to the myth that the peach tree of the Queen Mother bears fruit only once every three thousand years.
  • 此儿不良，已三过偷之，失王母意，故被谪来此 - Explains that the peach tree of the Queen Mother bears fruit only once every three thousand years, and this child has stolen it three times, offending the Queen Mother, and was therefore exiled.
  • 王母使人来告陛下，求道之法，惟有清静，不宜骚扰 - The Queen Mother sent someone to inform the emperor that the way to seek the Dao is through quietness and not disturbance.
  • 陛下自当知 - Says that the emperor will naturally know.
  • 朔与行道女子，并年百岁而死 - Sima Qian and the female practitioners live to be a hundred years old and then die.
  • 惟一女子，长陵徐氏号仪君，善传朔术 - Only one woman, Xu from Changling, known as Yi Jun, is skilled in passing on Sima Qian's techniques.
  • 至今上元延中，已百三十七岁矣，视之如童女 - By the time of the Yuan Dynasty, she has lived for one hundred and thirty-seven years and still looks like a young girl.
  • 者侯贵人更迎致之 - The noble lady sought to bring her to her.
  • 善行交接之道，无他法也 - She is skilled in the art of intercourse, and there is no other method.
  • 受道者皆与之通 - Those who learned the technique all had intercourse with her.
  • 或传，世淫之陈盛父子，皆与之行道 - It is said that the licentious Chen Sheng and his father all had intercourse with her.
  • 京中好淫乱者争就之 - Those in the capital who enjoy debauchery rushed to her.
  • 翟丞相奏坏风俗，请戮尤乱甚者 - Zhao the chancellor reported the bad customs and requested the execution of those who were especially corrupt.
  • 今上弗听 - The current emperor did not listen.
  • 乃徙女子于敦煌 - Then moved the woman to Dunhuang.
  • 后遂人胡，不知所终 - Later she went to the Hu people, and her fate is unknown.
  • 乐成侯上书，言方士栾大胶东人 - The Marquis of Lecheng submitted a memorial mentioning the Daoist master Luan Da from Jiaodong.
  • 故曾与文成侯同师 - Once studied with Wen Cheng Hou.
  • 上召见，大悦 - The emperor summoned him and was greatly pleased.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 朔 - Refers to Sima Qian, the famous historian and writer of the Western Han dynasty.
  • 短人 - A mythical figure often associated with dwarfs or short people in ancient Chinese literature.
  • 巨灵 - A mythical giant often associated with the creation of mountains and rivers.
  • 王母 - The Queen Mother of the West, a prominent figure in Chinese mythology, often associated with immortality and the peach of immortality.
  • 三千年一结子 - Refers to the myth that the peach tree of the Queen Mother bears fruit only once every three thousand years.
  • 文成侯 - A title given to a Daoist master who was favored by Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 栾大 - A Daoist master from Jiaodong who was also favored by Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 胶东 - A region in Shandong province, known for its historical significance.
  • 乐成侯 - A title given to a nobleman who submitted a memorial to the emperor.
  • 敦煌 - A city in Gansu province, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 胡 - Refers to the nomadic peoples of the northern steppes, often associated with the Xiongnu.

[216]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 大乃敢为大言，处之无疑 - Someone with great ambition dares to make grandiose claims and acts with complete confidence.
  • 处之无疑 - Literally 'acts with no doubt,' indicating absolute certainty or self-assurance.
  • 上乃封为乐通侯 - The emperor then enfeoffed him as the Marquis of Lü Tong, a formal title-granting ceremony.
  • 赐甲第、童奴千人，乘舆车马帷幄器物以克其家 - He was granted a grand mansion, a thousand servants, and all the vehicles, horses, tents, and utensils to match his status.
  • 更号当利公主 - He was given the title of Princess Dangli, indicating a change in his title or status.
  • 连年妖妄滋甚而不效 - Over the years, his false claims and magical practices became increasingly extravagant but failed to work.
  • 腰斩之 - He was executed by being cut in two at the waist, a form of execution in ancient China.
  • 发燕赵美女二千人充之 - Two thousand beautiful women from Yan and Zhao were sent to fill the palace.
  • 率皆十五以上二十以下，年满三十者出嫁之 - Most of them were between fifteen and twenty years old; those over thirty were married off.
  • 掖庭总籍，凡诸宫美女，万有八千 - The palace records showed that there were a total of 80,000 beautiful women in all the palaces.
  • 建章、未央、长安三宫，皆辇道相属 - The Jianzhang, Weiyang, and Chang'an palaces were all connected by carriage roads.
  • 率使宦者妇人分属，或以为仆射，大者领四五百，小者领一二百人 - They were generally managed by eunuchs and women, with some serving as overseers. The larger ones managed 400 to 500 people, while the smaller ones managed 100 to 200 people.
  • 常被幸御者则注其籍，增其俸秩，比六百石 - Those who were frequently favored by the emperor were recorded in the official register and received increased stipends, equivalent to the rank of 600 stones.
  • 宫人既多，极被幸者数年一再遇 - With so many palace women, those who were most favored saw the emperor only once or twice every few years.
  • 挟妇人媚术者甚众 - There were many who practiced women's charm techniques.
  • 选二百人，常从幸郡国 - Two hundred were selected to accompany him on his travels to the provinces.
  • 载之后车，与上同辇者十六人 - They were carried in the rear carriage, and sixteen of them shared the same carriage with the emperor.
  • 充数恒使满 - They were always kept at full strength.
  • 皆自然美丽，不假粉白黛绿 - All were naturally beautiful, without the need for makeup.
  • 侍尚衣轩者亦如之 - Those who served in the wardrobe pavilion were similarly beautiful.
  • 尝自言，能三日不食，不能一日无妇人 - He once said that he could go three days without food, but not one day without women.
  • 善行导养术，故体常壮悦 - He was skilled in health-preserving techniques, so his body remained strong and healthy.
  • 其应有子者，皆记其时日，赐金千斤 - Those who gave birth were recorded by date, and granted a thousand pounds of gold.
  • 孕者拜爵为容华，充侍衣之属 - Those who were pregnant were given the rank of Ronghua and assigned to serve in the wardrobe.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 其后 - Temporal marker meaning 'after that' or 'later.'
  • 上以夫人兄李广利为贰师将军，封海西侯，延年为协律都尉 - Series of actions taken by the emperor, including appointing R Li Guangli as the General of the Two Armies and bestowing the title of Marquis of Haixi, and appointing Yiannian as the Director of Music.
  • 上思念李夫人不已 - Phrase indicating the emperor's ongoing thoughts about Consort Li.
  • 方士齐人少翁言能致其神 - Statement that a Daoist from Qi named Shao Weng claims he can summon the spirit of Consort Li.
  • 夜张灯烛，设帷帐，陈酒肉，而令上居他帐，遥望见好女如李夫人之貌 - Description of the setup for the spirit-summoning ritual, including lighting lamps, setting up curtains, and arranging food and drink, with the emperor watching from another tent and seeing a beautiful woman resembling Consort Li.
  • 还幄坐而步，又不得就视 - Phrase indicating that the woman moved around inside the tent but the emperor could not get closer to see her clearly.
  • 上愈益相思悲感 - Phrase indicating that the emperor became even more sorrowful and pensive.
  • 为作诗曰 - Phrase indicating that the emperor composed a poem.
  • 是耶，非耶？立而望之，偏何姗姗其来迟 - Content of the poem, expressing uncertainty about whether the woman is Consort Li and lamenting her slow approach.
  • 令乐府诸音家弦歌之 - Command that the Music Bureau should have musicians perform the poem.
  • 上又自为作赋，以伤悼夫人 - Statement that the emperor also wrote an elegiac poem to mourn Consort Li.
  • 其辞曰 - Phrase indicating the beginning of the elegiac poem.
  • 美连娟以修兮，命要紧绝而不长 - Line of the poem describing Consort Li's beauty and lamenting her short life.
  • 饰新宫以延伫兮，泯不归乎故乡 - Line of the poem describing the new palace decorated for her, but she will never return to her homeland.
  • 惨郁郁其芜秽兮，处幽隐而怀伤 - Line of the poem describing the desolate and overgrown state of the palace, with the emperor feeling sorrowful in seclusion.
  • 释舆马于山椒兮，奄修夜之不阳 - Line of the poem describing the release of carriages and horses on a mountain peak, and the long, dark night without sunlight.
  • 秋气惨以凄泪兮，桂枝落而销亡 - Line of the poem describing the bleak autumn weather, with the falling of osmanthus branches symbolizing her death.
  • 神莺茕以遥思兮，精浮游而出疆 - Line of the poem describing the spirit of the phoenix, lonely and distant, floating beyond the borders.
  • 托沉阴以旷久兮，借蕃番华之未央 - Line of the poem describing the long, dark period of mourning, with the fragrance of flowers still lingering.
  • 念穷极之不还兮，惟幼眇之相羊 - Line of the poem expressing the emperor's thoughts on her not returning, and his wandering in grief.
  • 函以俟风兮，芳杂袭以弥章 - Line of the poem describing the waiting for the wind, with the fragrance of flowers becoming more intense.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 容与以猗靡兮 - Describes a graceful and composed movement.
  • 缥飘姚乎愈庄 - Describes a movement that is both graceful and increasingly solemn.
  • 燕淫衍而抚楹兮 - Describes a scene where something spreads luxuriantly and touches the pillars.
  • 连流视而娥扬 - Describes a gaze that is both flowing and graceful.
  • 既激感而心逐兮 - Describes a state where the heart is already stirred and follows the movement.
  • 包红颜而弗明 - Describes a state where beauty is enveloped but not clearly visible.
  • 欢接狎以离别兮 - Describes a scene of joyfully meeting and then separating.
  • 宵寤梦之茫茫 - Describes a state of awakening at night with vague dreams.
  • 忽迁化而不返兮 - Describes a sudden transformation that does not return.
  • 魂放逸以飞扬 - Describes a soul that is wandering and soaring.
  • 何灵魂之纷纷兮 - Describes a state of scattered souls.
  • 哀裴回以踌躇 - Describes a state of sadly wandering and hesitating.
  • 势路日以远兮 - Describes a path of fate that is growing distant.
  • 遂荒忽而辞去 - Describes a state where something becomes vague and departs.
  • 超兮西征，屑兮不见 - Describes a scene where something soars westward and fades away.
  • 浸淫敞恍，寂兮无音 - Describes a state that gradually becomes dim and silent.
  • 思若流波，怛兮在心 - Describes thoughts that are like flowing waves and grievous in the heart.
  • 乱曰 - A formal way to introduce a concluding statement or summary.
  • 佳侠函光，陨朱荣兮 - Describes an excellent and heroic figure whose glory falls.
  • 嫉妒癔茸，将安程兮 - Describes a state of jealousy and confusion, asking how to proceed.
  • 方时隆盛，年夭伤兮 - Describes a state of prosperity that is short-lived and injured.
  • 弟子增欷，沫怅兮 - Describes disciples sighing deeply and being sorrowful.
  • 悲愁于邑，喧不可止兮 - Describes sorrow and grief in the city that is uncontrollable.
  • 向不虚应，亦云已矣 - Describes a state where something was not responded to in vain but is now over.
  • 礁妍太息，叹稚子兮 - Describes a beautiful and graceful figure sighing for the young.
  • 恸栗不言，倚所恃兮 - Describes a deeply shaken figure who is silent and relies on what is relied upon.
  • 仁者不誓，岂约亲兮 - Describes a benevolent person who does not swear and wonders how it can be close.
  • 既往不来，申以信兮 - Describes a state where once something is gone, it does not return, and trust is expressed.
  • 去彼昭昭，就冥冥兮 - Describes a state where something leaves the bright and approaches the dark.
  • 既下新宫，不复故庭兮 - Describes a state where something has descended to the new palace and no longer returns to the old courtyard.
  • 呜呼哀哉，想魂灵兮 - Describes a state of lamenting and thinking of the soul.
  • 其后，李延年弟季，坐奸乱后宫，广利降匈奴，家族灭矣 - Describes a later event where Li Yan's younger brother Ji was accused of corruption in the harem, Guangli surrendered to the Xiongnu, and the family was destroyed.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李延年 - A famous musician and courtier during the Han dynasty, known for his music and influence at court.
  • 后宫 - Refers to the harem or the inner palace where the emperor's concubines and attendants lived.
  • 广利 - A general during the Han dynasty, known for his military campaigns against the Xiongnu.
  • 匈奴 - The Xiongnu, a nomadic confederation that was a major adversary of the Han dynasty.
  • 家族灭矣 - Refers to the destruction of a family, which was a common punishment for treason or corruption during the Han dynasty.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The opening phrase '容与以猗靡兮，缥飘姚乎愈庄' describes a graceful and composed movement that becomes increasingly solemn.
  • The phrase '燕淫衍而抚楹兮，连流视而娥扬' describes a scene where something spreads luxuriantly and touches the pillars, with a flowing and graceful gaze.
  • The phrase '既激感而心逐兮，包红颜而弗明' describes a state where the heart is already stirred and follows the movement, but the beauty is enveloped and not clearly visible.
  • The phrase '欢接狎以离别兮，宵寤梦之茫茫' describes a scene of joyfully meeting and then separating, followed by awakening at night with vague dreams.
  • The phrase '忽迁化而不返兮' describes a sudden transformation that does not return.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 武帝思懷往者李夫人不可得復時 - Emperor Wu longed for Consort Li, who had passed away, and could not reunite with her.
  • 始穿昆灵之池，泛翔禽之舟 - He began to dig the Pool of Kunling and floated a boat for birds.
  • 帝自造歌曲，使女伶歌之 - The emperor himself created songs and had female musicians sing them.
  • 羅袂兮無聲，玉墀兮塵生 - Silk sleeves are silent, jade steps are covered in dust.
  • 虛房冷而寂寞，落葉依於重扃 - The empty room is cold and lonely, fallen leaves cling to the heavy door.
  • 望彼美之女兮，安得感余心之未寧 - Looking at that beautiful woman, how can I soothe my restless heart?
  • 特命龍膏之燭以照舟內，悲不自止 - He especially commanded candles made from dragon fat to light the boat, unable to stop grieving.
  • 親侍者覺帝容色愁怨，乃進洪梁之酒，酌以文螺之卮 - The attendants noticed the emperor's sorrowful expression and offered wine from Hongliang, served in a cup made from a shell.
  • 卮出波祗之國，酒出洪梁之縣 - The cup came from the country of Bozhi, and the wine came from the county of Hongliang.
  • 帝飲三爵，色悅心歡，乃詔女伶出侍，帝息於延涼室 - The emperor drank three cups, his face brightened, and his heart was happy, so he ordered the female musicians to attend, and he rested in the Cool Hall.
  • 帝驚起，而香氣猶著衣枕，歷月不歇 - The emperor was startled awake, and the fragrance still lingered on his clothes and pillow, lasting for months.
  • 涕泣洽席，遂改延涼室為遺芳夢室 - Tears wet the seat, and he changed the Cool Hall into the Hall of Lingering Fragrance and Dream.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 武帝 - Emperor Wu of Han, a famous emperor of the Han dynasty known for his military campaigns and cultural patronage.
  • 李夫人 - Consort Li, a beloved concubine of Emperor Wu, who died before him and is often associated with themes of longing and loss in literature.
  • 昆灵之池 - Pool of Kunling, likely a symbolic or metaphorical name for a pool or pond, possibly named after the mythical Kunpeng (a giant bird from Daoist mythology).
  • 翔禽之舟 - A boat designed for birds, possibly a metaphor for a luxurious or ornate vessel.
  • 龍膏之燭 - Candles made from dragon fat, symbolizing luxury and rarity.
  • 洪梁之酒 - Wine from the county of Hongliang, likely known for its quality.
  • 文螺之卮 - A wine cup made from a type of shell, possibly named 'wenluo' (literally 'literary shell'), symbolizing elegance and refinement.
  • 波祗之國 - A country named Bozhi, possibly a fictional or symbolic name.
  • 洪梁之縣 - A county named Hongliang, likely known for producing high-quality wine.
  • 延涼室 - A hall named 'Cool Hall,' possibly a place of rest or relaxation.
  • 遺芳夢室 - A hall named 'Hall of Lingering Fragrance and Dream,' likely named in memory of Consort Li and the lingering fragrance of her presence.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 歌畢 - Literally means 'after singing is finished,' indicating the completion of a song or chant.
  • 未欲修身，當先營其氣 - A philosophical statement suggesting that before one can cultivate the body, one must first nurture the vital energy (氣).
  • 大仙真經 - Refers to a canonical text or scripture associated with Taoist or spiritual teachings.
  • 行益者益精，行易者易形 - A complex phrase suggesting that practicing the way of benefit increases essence, while practicing the way of ease transforms the form.
  • 能易能益，名上仙籍 - Means that those who can transform and increase their essence are recorded in the registry of immortals.
  • 不益不易，不離死厄 - Suggests that without transformation and increase, one cannot escape the fate of death.
  • 行益易者，謂常思念靈寶 - Means that the practice of benefit and ease involves constantly thinking about the 'Spiritual Treasure' (靈寶).
  • 靈者，神也。寶者，精也 - Explains that 'Spiritual' (靈) refers to the divine or spiritual, and 'Treasure' (寶) refers to essence or vital energy.
  • 子但愛精握固，闲气吞液 - Means that one should cherish essence, hold it firmly, and control the breath while absorbing the essence of liquids.
  • 气化为血，血化为精，精化为神，神化为液，液化为骨 - Describes a series of transformations from qi (vital energy) to blood, essence, spirit, liquid, and finally bone.
  • 行之不倦，神精充溢 - Means that by practicing this without tiring, the spirit and essence will become abundant.
  • 为之一年易气，二年易血，三年易精，四年易脉，五年易髓，六年易筋，七年易骨，八年易发，九年易形 - Describes a nine-year process of transformation, each year focusing on a different aspect of the body.
  • 易形则变化，变化则成道，成道则为仙人 - Suggests that transforming the form leads to change, change leads to the Way, and the Way leads to becoming an immortal.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 诸仙佩之，皆如转章 - All the immortals wear it, as if it were a revolving chapter. This phrase suggests that the object in question is something that the immortals use or possess, and it has a dynamic or transformative quality.
  • 道士执之，经行山川 - The Taoist priests hold it, traveling through mountains and rivers. This indicates the object's utility in spiritual or physical journeys.
  • 百神群灵，尊奉亲近 - All the gods and spirits revere and approach it closely. This implies that the object is highly revered and sought after by supernatural beings.
  • 汝虽不敏，然诣仙泽叩求，不忌于道 - Though you are not very intelligent, you have come to the spring of immortals to seek it, without fear of the path. This acknowledges the subject's lack of intelligence but praises their courage and determination.
  • 欣子有心，今以相与，当深奉敬，如事君父，泄失示人，必祸及也 - I am glad that you have the heart to seek it. Now I will give it to you. You should deeply revere it, as you would serve your lord or father. If you reveal it to others, disaster will surely befall you. This conveys the importance of the object and the consequences of its misuse.
  • 阿母今以琼航笈妙韫，发紫台之文，赐汝八会之书 - Mother now gives you the precious scroll containing the hidden treasures, revealing the writings of the Purple Platform, and bestows upon you the Eight Assemblies' scriptures. This describes the gift given to the subject, emphasizing its value and significance.
  • 五岳真形，可谓至真且贵，上帝之玄观也 - The true forms of the Five Great Mountains are truly the most genuine and precious, the profound vision of the Supreme Deity. This highlights the spiritual and divine nature of the Five Great Mountains.
  • 子自非受命合神，焉见此文 - You would not have seen these writings unless you were destined to receive them. This suggests that the subject's ability to see the writings is a sign of divine favor.
  • 今虽得其形，观其妙理，而无五帝六甲，左右灵飞之符，太阴六丁，通真逐灵玉女之篆，太阳六戊，招神天光策精之书；左乙混洞东蒙之文，右庚素招摄杀之律；壬癸六远，隐地八术、丙丁八大九赤班符；六辛人金致黄水月华之法，六巳石精金光藏景化形之方；子午卯西，八禀十决，六灵威仪；丑辰未戊，地真曲素诀辞，长生紫书，三五顺行；寅已申亥，紫度炎光，内视中方 - This long sequence lists various esoteric texts, symbols, and rituals required to fully understand and utilize the Five Great Mountains' true forms. Each item represents a specific aspect of Taoist practice and knowledge.
  • 凡缺此十二事者，当何以召山灵、朝地神、摄万精、驱百鬼、束虎豹、役蚊龙乎 - Without these twelve things, how can you summon mountain spirits, pay homage to earth deities, control myriad spirits, drive away a hundred demons, tame tigers and leopards, or command dragons and snakes? This emphasizes the necessity of having all the listed items to achieve the desired spiritual and magical effects.
  • 子所谓通，知其一，未见其他 - You claim to be knowledgeable, but you only know one thing, not the others. This criticizes the subject for being superficial in their understanding.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This phrase is straightforward but must be read as a single action of sealing and sending a document.
  • This phrase is a concessive statement, acknowledging Chè's intention but denying his suitability for immortality.
  • This is a rhetorical question, emphasizing the inappropriate nature of the action.
  • This phrase introduces a new subject and must be read as a complete thought about Chāng's proximity to the emperor and the many who have spoken to him.
  • This phrase uses parallel structure to describe the consequences of improper actions, with '云山鬼笑' and '孤魂号' forming a balanced pair.
  • This phrase also uses parallel structure, with '舆师归而族有功' and '忘兵劳而纵白骨' forming a balanced pair.
  • This phrase is a list of negative actions, with '烦扰黔首' and '淫酷自恣' forming a balanced pair.
  • This phrase is a list of consequences, with '罪已彰于太上' and '怨已见于天气' forming a balanced pair.
  • This phrase is a conditional statement, with '嚣言玄闻' and '必不得度世也' forming a logical sequence.
  • This phrase is a simple statement of obedience, with '奉尊见敕' and '不敢违耳' forming a logical sequence.
  • This phrase is a concessive statement, with '言此子者诚多' and '然帝亦不必推也' forming a logical sequence.
  • This phrase is a simple statement of Chè's dedication, with '彻念道累年' and '斋亦勤矣' forming a logical sequence.
  • This phrase is a simple statement of Chè's prayers, with '累祷名山' and '愿求度脱' forming a logical sequence.
  • This phrase is a simple statement of balance, with '较计功过' and '殆已相掩' forming a logical sequence.
  • This phrase is a simple statement of future action, with '但自今以去' and '勤修至诚' forming a logical sequence.
  • This phrase is a simple statement of guidance, with '奉上元夫人之言' and '不宜复奢淫暴虐' forming a logical sequence.
  • This phrase is a complex statement of consequences, with '使万兆劳残' and '冤魂穷鬼有破屋之诉' forming a balanced pair, and '流血之尸' and '忘功赏之辞耳' forming another balanced pair.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此三天太上之所撰 - Refers to the creation of the text by the Three Supreme Ones of Heaven, the highest deities in Taoist cosmology.
  • 藏于紫陵之台 - Indicates that the text is hidden on the Purple Terrace of Purity, a sacred place for storing divine texts.
  • 隐以灵坛之房 - Refers to the text being hidden in a sacred chamber on a spirit altar, a place of divine protection.
  • 封以华琳之函 - Describes the text being sealed in a box of exquisite jade, symbolizing its preciousness and sanctity.
  • 韫以兰简之帛 - Refers to the text being wrapped in silk from the orchid bamboo, a high-quality, fragrant silk used for important documents.
  • 约以紫罗之索 - Indicates the text being bound with purple silk rope, another symbol of its sacred nature.
  • 印以大帝之玺 - Describes the text being sealed with the imperial seal of the Great Emperor, indicating the highest authority and divine sanction.
  • 受之者四十年传一人 - Sets a rule for the transmission of the text, stating that it is transmitted to one person every forty years.
  • 无其人，八十年可授二人 - Provides a fallback rule, stating that if no suitable person is found, the text can be transmitted to two people every eighty years.
  • 得道者四百年一传 - Indicates that those who attain the Way transmit the text every four hundred years.
  • 得仙者四千年一传 - Refers to those who become immortals transmitting the text every four thousand years.
  • 得真者四万年一传 - Indicates that those who achieve true enlightenment transmit the text every forty thousand years.
  • 升太上者四十万年一传 - Refers to those who ascend to the highest level transmitting the text every four hundred thousand years.
  • 非其人，谓之泄天道 - States that if the text is not given to the right person, it is called leaking the heavenly way, a serious sin.
  • 得其人不传，是谓蔽天宝 - Indicates that if the text is given to the right person but not transmitted, it is called concealing the heavenly treasure, another serious sin.
  • 非限妄传，是谓轻人老 - Refers to transmitting the text outside the limits, which is called disrespecting the ancient sages, indicating a lack of respect for tradition.
  • 受而不敬，是谓慢天藻 - Indicates that receiving the text without reverence is called disrespecting the heavenly law, showing a lack of proper respect.
  • 泄、蔽、轻、慢四者，取死之刀斧，延祸之车乘也 - Describes the four violations as being like the executioner's axe and the chariot of disaster, emphasizing the severe consequences.
  • 泄者，身死于道路，受土刑而骸裂 - States that those who leak the text will die on the road and suffer earthy punishment, causing their bones to split.
  • 蔽者，盲聋于求世，命调残而卒殁 - Indicates that those who conceal the text will be blind and deaf in seeking the world, and their life will be shortened and they will die prematurely.
  • 轻则祸钟于父母，诣玄都而受罚 - Refers to disrespecting the ancient sages bringing disaster to one's parents, and one will go to the Xuan Du (Heavenly Capital) to receive punishment.
  • 慢则暴终而堕恶，生弃疾于后世 - Indicates that being disrespectful will lead to a sudden and violent end, falling into evil, and leaving disease for future generations.
  • 此皆道之科禁，故以相戒，不可不慎也 - Summarizes the rules and prohibitions of the Way, emphasizing the need for caution.
  • 王母因授以五岳真形图 - Describes the Queen Mother bestowing the True Forms of the Five Peaks Chart, a sacred map in Taoist tradition.
  • 帝拜受俱毕 - Indicates that the emperor bowed and received the text, and then both of them finished the ceremony.
  • 夫人自弹云林之，歌步玄之曲 - Refers to the lady playing the Cloud Forest melody and singing the Step into the Void song, a musical performance.
  • 王母命侍女田四非答歌 - Indicates that the Queen Mother commanded her servant Tian Sini to respond with a song, a reciprocal musical exchange.
  • 歌毕，乃告帝从者姓名，及冠带执佩物名 - Describes the lady telling the names of the emperor's attendants and the names of their hats, belts, and ornaments, providing detailed information.
  • 所以得知而纪焉 - Emphasizes the importance of documentation, so that it could be known and recorded.
  • 及明，王母与上元夫人同乘而去 - Indicates the departure of the Queen Mother and the Lady of the Upper Yuan, who rode away together at dawn.
  • 龙虎车马导从音乐如初来时 - Describes the grandeur of the departure, with dragon and tiger chariots, horses, and music following as when they first arrived.
  • 云彩郁勃，尽为香气 - Describes a mystical transformation, where the clouds were dense and turned into fragrant incense.

  • 后月余，乃立婕妤为皇后 - '后月余' means 'after a month'; '乃立婕妤为皇后' means 'then made her the empress.'
  • 追以长前白罢昌陵功，封为定陵侯 - '追以长前白罢昌陵功' means 'posthumously honored Chunyu Chang for his earlier role in abandoning the construction of Changling'; '封为定陵侯' means 'and enfeoffed him as Marquis of Dingling.'
  • 皇后既立，后宠少衰，而弟绝幸，为昭仪，居昭阳舍 - '皇后既立' means 'after she became empress'; '后宠少衰' means 'her favor waned'; '而弟绝幸' means 'but her younger sister was greatly favored'; '为昭仪' means 'became a *Zhao Yi* (a high-ranking concubine)'; '居昭阳舍' means 'lived in the *Zhaoyang Palace*.'
  • 其中庭彤朱，而殿上髹漆，砌皆铜沓冒黄金，涂白玉，阶壁带往往为黄金，函蓝田璧，明珠翠羽饰之 - '其中庭彤朱' means 'the courtyard was colored with red and cinnabar'; '而殿上髹漆' means 'and the palace hall was painted with lacquer'; '砌皆铜沓冒黄金' means 'the steps were covered with gold-plated copper'; '涂白玉' means 'coated with white jade'; '阶壁带往往为黄金' means 'the walls and steps were often adorned with gold'; '函蓝田璧，明珠翠羽饰之' means 'the walls were inlaid with Lan Tian jade and decorated with pearls and green feathers.'
  • 自后宫未尝有焉 - Means 'such luxury had never been seen in the harem before.'
  • 姊弟专宠十余年，卒皆无子 - '姊弟专宠十余年' means 'the sisters enjoyed exclusive favor for over ten years'; '卒皆无子' means 'but neither of them had children.'
  • 未年，定陶王来朝 - '未年' means 'in the last year of the emperor's reign'; '定陶王来朝' means 'the Prince of Dingtao came to court.'
  • 王祖母傅太后私赂遗赵皇后、昭仪，定陶王竟为太子 - '王祖母傅太后私赂遗赵皇后、昭仪' means 'the prince's grandmother, Empress Dowager Fu, secretly bribed Zhao Empress and the *Zhao Yi*'; '定陶王竟为太子' means 'the Prince of Dingtao was eventually made crown prince.'
  • 明年春，成帝崩 - '明年春' means 'the following spring'; '成帝崩' means 'Emperor Cheng died.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 孝成赵皇后 - Zhao Empress of Emperor Cheng, a significant figure in the Western Han dynasty.
  • 孝成 - The posthumous name of Emperor Cheng of Han.
  • 阳阿主 - Yang E Princess, a member of the imperial family who trained Zhao Feiyan in music and dance.
  • 婕妤 - A high-ranking concubine in the imperial harem.
  • 皇太后 - The empress dowager, typically the mother of the emperor.

  • 昌陵 - The planned new capital city during the reign of Emperor Cheng.
  • 定陶王 - The Prince of Dingtao, who later became Emperor Ai of Han.
  • 傅太后 - Empress Dowager Fu, the grandmother of the Prince of Dingtao.

Proper Nouns

  • 艳异编卷七宫掖部三 - The title of the text, indicating it is the third part of the seventh volume of *Yan Yi Bian* (《艳异编》), which is a collection of strange and unusual stories, focusing on the palace and harem sections.
  • 赵婕妤 - Zhao Jieyu, a high-ranking concubine who later became empress.

  • 昭仪 - A high-ranking concubine in the imperial harem.
  • 昭阳舍 - The residence of Zhao Hede in the imperial harem.
  • 明珠 - Pearls, used in the decoration of the *Zhaoyang Palace*.
  • 翠羽 - Green feathers, used in the decoration of the *Zhaoyang Palace*.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 帝素强无疾病 - Literally 'the emperor was always strong and had no illness,' implying that his death was unexpected.
  • 明旦当辞去 - Means 'the next day they will depart,' indicating a formal visit.
  • 上宿供张白虎殿 - Refers to the emperor's stay and the preparations made for his reception in the White Tiger Hall.
  • 欲拜左将军孔光为丞相 - Means 'he intended to appoint General Zuo Kong Guang as Chancellor,' showing a political decision.
  • 已刻侯印书赞 - Means 'the seal for the marquisate had already been engraved and the commendation written,' indicating preparations for the appointment.
  • 昏夜平善，乡晨傅裤袜欲起 - Means 'he was well into the night and at dawn, he put on his trousers and socks and wanted to get up,' describing the emperor's routine.
  • 因失衣不能言 - Means 'he lost his voice and could not speak,' indicating a sudden onset of illness.
  • 昼漏下十刻而崩 - Means 'at the tenth watch of the day, he died,' referring to the time of death.
  • 民间归罪赵昭仪 - Means 'the people blamed Zhao Zhaoyi,' indicating public sentiment.
  • 皇太后诏大司马莽、丞相大司空曰 - Means 'the Empress Dowager issued an edict to the Grand Marshal Ma, the Chancellor, and the Grand Administrator,' showing the official response.
  • 皇帝暴崩，群众喧哗怪之 - Means 'the emperor died suddenly, and the people were startled and chattered,' indicating public reaction.
  • 掖庭令辅等在后庭，左右侍燕迫近 - Means 'the eunuch Fu and others were in the inner palace, close to the emperor,' describing the setting.
  • 杂与御史丞相廷尉治间皇帝起居发病状 - Means 'they mixed with the Imperial Censor, the Chancellor, and the Grand Judge to investigate the emperor's daily life and the onset of illness,' indicating the investigation.
  • 赵昭仪自杀 - Means 'Zhao Zhaoyi committed suicide,' showing the outcome of the investigation.
  • 哀帝既立，尊赵皇后为皇太后 - Means 'after the Emperor Ai ascended the throne, he honored Zhao as Empress Dowager,' indicating a change in status.
  • 封太后弟侍中驸马都尉钦为新成侯 - Means 'he bestowed the title of Xin Cheng Marquis on her brother, who served as a Privy Councillor and Marquis of the Imperial Stables,' showing a reward.
  • 赵氏侯者凡二人 - Means 'there were two Zhao marquises,' indicating the extent of the reward.
  • 后数月，司隶解光奏言 - Means 'several months later, the Inspector of the Commandery Jie Guang presented a report,' showing the continuation of events.
  • 臣闻许美人及故中宫史曹宫，皆御幸孝成皇帝，产子，子隐不见 - Means 'I heard that Xu Meiren and the former palace historian Cao Gong, both favored by Emperor Cheng, had children who were hidden and not seen,' indicating a scandal.
  • 臣遣从事椽业史望，验问知状者 - Means 'I sent my subordinate Ying Shi Wang to verify the accounts of those who knew the details,' showing the investigation.
  • 掖庭狱丞籍武，故中黄门王舜、吴恭、靳严，宫婢曹晓、道房、张弃，故赵昭仪御者于客子、王偏、臧兼等 - Lists the individuals involved in the investigation.
  • 皆曰：宫即晓子女，前属中宫为学事史，通《诗》，授皇后 - Means 'they all said: Gong was the daughter of Xiao, previously assigned to the palace as a historian, proficient in the *Book of Songs*, and taught the Empress,' providing background.
  • 房与宫对食 - Means 'Fang and Gong ate together,' indicating a relationship.
  • 元延元年，中宫语房曰：陛下幸宫 - Means 'in the first year of Yuanyan, the Empress said to Fang: 'The Emperor favored Gong,' indicating a relationship.
  • 后数月，晓人殿中，见宫腹大，问宫。宫曰：‘御幸有娠 - Means 'several months later, Xiao entered the palace and saw Gong's large belly, asking her. Gong said: 'The Emperor favored me and I am pregnant,' indicating the pregnancy.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 宫乳掖庭 - Refers to the imperial palace, specifically the area where the emperor's concubines and their children lived.
  • 牛官令舍 - Refers to the residence of the official in charge of cattle.
  • 中黄门田客持诏记盛绿绨方底 - Describes a messenger (中黄门田客) holding an imperial edict (诏记) written on green silk (绿绨方底).
  • 封御史中丞印予武曰 - Indicates that the edict was sealed with the seal of the Grand Censor (御史中丞) and given to Wu, who is likely a subordinate.
  • 取牛官令舍妇人新产儿、婢六人，尽置暴室狱，毋问儿男女、谁儿也 - Orders the seizure of a new mother and her child, along with six maids, and their placement in a prison (暴室狱), without regard to the child's gender or identity.
  • 武迎置狱 - Wu complies with the order and places the individuals in the prison.
  • 善藏我儿胞，丞知是何等儿也 - The mother requests that Wu hide her child's umbilical cord, implying that she knows something about the child's identity.
  • 后三日，客持诏记与武，问：‘儿死未？手书对犊背。’ - Three days later, the messenger returns with another edict, asking if the child is dead, and instructs Wu to write the answer on the back of a calf (犊背).
  • 儿见在未死 - Wu writes that the child is still alive.
  • 上与昭仪大怒，奈何不杀 - The emperor and the empress dowager are furious, questioning why the child was not killed.
  • 武叩头啼曰：‘不杀儿自知当死，杀之亦死 - Wu pleads, stating that if he does not kill the child, he will die, and if he does, he will also die.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 美人以苇箧一合 - A beautiful lady placed a basket of woven reeds together.

  • 及绿囊报书予严 - And a green bag with a report letter to Yan.
  • 严持筐书置饰室帘南去 - Yan took the basket and the letter and placed them south of the curtain in the decorated room.
  • 帝与昭仪坐 - The emperor sat with the empress.
  • 使客子解箧缄 - He ordered a guest to open the basket.
  • 未已，帝使客子、偏、兼皆出 - Before it was finished, the emperor ordered the guest, the attendant, and the subordinate to leave.
  • 自闭户，独与昭仪在 - He closed the door himself and was left alone with the empress.
  • 须臾开户 - After a short while, he opened the door.
  • 呼客子、偏、兼，使缄封蘑及绿绨方底 - He called the guest, the attendant, and the subordinate to seal the mushroom and the green silk square bottom.
  • 推置屏风东 - Pushed them east of the screen.
  • 恭受诏，持箧方底予武 - The respectful one received the order and held the basket and the square bottom to Wu.
  • 皆封以御史中丞印 - Sealed them with the seal of the Grand Censor.
  • 告武，箧中有死儿埋屏处，勿令人知 - Instruct Wu, the basket contains a dead child buried behind the screen, do not let anyone know.
  • 武穿狱楼垣下为坎，埋其中 - Wu dug a pit under the wall of the prison building and buried the child in it.
  • 故长定许贵人及故成都平阿侯家婢王业、任、公孙习前免为庶人，诏召入，属昭仪为私婢 - The former Lady Xu of Changding and the former maidservant Wang Ye, Ren, and Gongsun Xi of the former Ping'a Marquis of Chengdu, who were previously demoted to commoners, were summoned by decree and assigned to the empress as her private maidservants.
  • 成帝崩，未幸梓宫仓卒悲哀之，时昭仪自知罪恶大，知业等故许氏、王氏婢，恐事泄，而以大婢羊子等赐予业等各且十人以慰其意，嘱‘勿道我家过失’ - After Emperor Cheng died, before he was laid in the coffin, in the sudden grief, the empress knew her own great sins, knowing that Ye and others were former servants of the Xu and Wang families, fearing that the matter would be exposed, she gave the maidservants Yangzi and others, about ten each, to Ye and others to comfort them, instructing them, 'Do not speak of my family's faults.'
  • 元延二年，故掖庭令吾丘遵谓武曰 - In the second year of Yuanyan, the former chief of the palace eunuchs, Wuqiu Zun, said to Wu.
  • 掖丞庭吏以下，皆与昭仪合通无可与语者，独欲与武有所言 - The officials of the palace eunuch's office and below, all had relations with the empress and had no one to talk to, but wanted to say something to Wu.
  • 我无子，武有子，是家轻族人，得无不敢乎 - I have no children, but Wu has children, this family is light on relatives, could it be that you dare not?
  • 掖庭中御幸生子者辄死，又饮药伤堕者无数，欲与武共言之大臣 - In the palace eunuch's office, those who were favored by the emperor and gave birth to children died, and there were countless who drank medicine and miscarried, wanting to talk about it with the ministers.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 美人 - A title for a high-ranking imperial concubine.
  • 昭仪 - A title for a high-ranking imperial concubine.
  • 御史中丞 - The title of the Grand Censor, a high-ranking official.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 骠骑将军贪嗜钱 - Describes the general's excessive greed for money.
  • 不足计事 - Means 'not worth considering for important matters.'
  • 奈何令长信得闻之 - A rhetorical question meaning 'how can we let the Empress Dowager know about it?'
  • 遵后病困，谓武 - Indicates that after Zun fell ill, he told Wu.
  • 前所语事，武不能独为也 - Means 'the things we discussed before, Wu cannot do alone.'
  • 慎语 - Means 'be careful about speaking.'
  • 皆在今年四月赦令前 - Means 'all these things happened before the amnesty decree of April this year.'
  • 臣谨按 - A formal expression meaning 'I respectfully report.'
  • 永光三年，男子忠等发长陵傅夫人冢 - Refers to an incident in the third year of Yong Guang where men named Zhong et al. desecrated the tomb of Lady Fu at Changling.
  • 比朕不当所得赦也 - A direct quote from Emperor Yuan's edict, meaning 'compared to my own actions, these should not be pardoned.'
  • 穷治，尽伏辜 - Means 'thoroughly investigated, all were punished according to their crimes.'
  • 天下以为当 - Means 'the whole country considered it appropriate.'
  • 鲁严公夫人杀世子，齐桓召而诛焉，《春秋》予之 - Refers to a historical event where the wife of Duke Yan of Lu killed his eldest son, and Duke Huan of Qi summoned and punished her, which was praised in the *Spring and Autumn Annals*.
  • 赵昭仪倾乱圣朝，亲灭继嗣，家属当伏天诛 - Describes the actions of Zhao Zhaoyi, who disrupted the court and personally extinguished the line of succession, leading to the punishment of her family.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 长陵 - The mausoleum of Emperor Gaozu of Han, located near modern-day Xi'an.
  • 傅夫人 - Lady Fu, a noblewoman buried in Changling.
  • 孝元皇帝 - Emperor Yuan of Han.
  • 鲁严公夫人 - The wife of Duke Yan of Lu, a historical figure mentioned in the *Spring and Autumn Annals*.
  • 齐桓 - Duke Huan of Qi, a prominent ruler during the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 赵昭仪 - Zhao Zhaoyi, a concubine of Emperor Cheng of Han who had significant influence and was involved in political intrigues.
  • 谒坐大逆 - A serious crime involving disrespect to the throne or the emperor.
  • 同产 - Refers to relatives by blood or marriage.
  • 故郡 - The original fief or place of residence.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 昭儀 - A high-ranking concubine title in the imperial harem.
  • 悖逆 - Means 'rebellious' or 'treacherous,' often used in the context of disloyalty to the emperor.
  • 同產 - Refers to siblings or close relatives.
  • 帷幄 - Literally 'curtain and tent,' used metaphorically to refer to the inner chambers of the palace where important decisions are made.
  • 群下寒心 - Means 'all officials are filled with fear,' indicating a loss of trust and respect among the court.
  • 窮竟 - Means 'to thoroughly investigate' or 'to bring to a conclusion.'
  • 正法 - Refers to the legal punishment or execution according to the law.
  • 免新成侯趙欽，欽兄子成陽侯訴，皆為庶人 - Indicates that both Suiqing and his nephew Suyu were stripped of their titles and reduced to commoners.
  • 徙遼西郡 - Means 'exiled to Liaoxi Commandery,' indicating a severe punishment.
  • 泰伯 - A legendary figure in Chinese history, known for his humility and respect for tradition.
  • 見歷知適 - Means 'to foresee the rightful heir,' referring to the ability to recognize the legitimate successor.
  • 逡循固讓 - Means 'to humbly decline' or 'to yield graciously,' describing the act of refusing a position of power.
  • 權變所設 - Means 'a strategic move,' indicating a deliberate action taken for a specific purpose.
  • 不計常法 - Means 'not bound by conventional rules,' suggesting that the action was exceptional and beyond normal norms.
  • 致位王季 - Means 'to elevate Wang Ji to a position of power,' referring to the elevation of Wang Ji, the younger brother of King Tai.
  • 以崇聖嗣 - Means 'to honor the rightful heir,' emphasizing the importance of recognizing the legitimate successor.
  • 七八百載 - Means 'several hundred years,' indicating a long period of time.
  • 功冠三王 - Means 'his achievements surpass those of three kings,' praising the legendary figure's accomplishments.
  • 道德最備 - Means 'his moral character is most complete,' highlighting the high moral standards of the legendary figure.
  • 是以尊號追及太王 - Means 'therefore, his posthumous title was bestowed upon Taiwang,' indicating the recognition of his contributions after death.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 昭儀 - A high-ranking concubine title in the imperial harem, often associated with significant influence and power.
  • 悖逆 - A term often used in the context of disloyalty to the emperor, reflecting the severe consequences of such actions.
  • 同產 - Refers to siblings or close relatives, emphasizing the family connections and the impact of the crime on the family.
  • 帷幄 - Metaphorically refers to the inner chambers of the palace where important decisions are made, indicating the high level of trust and responsibility.
  • 群下寒心 - Indicates a loss of trust and respect among the court, reflecting the severity of the situation.
  • 窮竟 - Refers to a thorough investigation, emphasizing the seriousness of the case.
  • 正法 - Refers to the legal punishment or execution according to the law, indicating the severity of the crime.
  • 免新成侯趙欽，欽兄子成陽侯訴，皆為庶人 - Indicates the severe punishment of stripping titles and reducing to commoners, reflecting the gravity of the crime.
  • 徙遼西郡 - Indicates the severe punishment of exile to a remote commandery, further emphasizing the severity of the crime.
  • 泰伯 - A legendary figure in Chinese history, known for his humility and respect for tradition, often cited as a model of virtue.
  • 見歷知適 - Refers to the ability to recognize the rightful heir, emphasizing the importance of recognizing the legitimate successor.
  • 逡循固讓 - Describes the act of humbly declining a position of power, reflecting the importance of humility and respect for tradition.
  • 權變所設 - Indicates a strategic move, suggesting that the action was deliberate and purposeful.
  • 不計常法 - Suggests that the action was exceptional and beyond normal norms, reflecting the unique circumstances.
  • 致位王季 - Refers to the elevation of Wang Ji, the younger brother of King Tai, to a position of power, emphasizing the importance of recognizing the rightful heir.
  • 以崇聖嗣 - Emphasizes the importance of recognizing the legitimate successor, reflecting the moral and political significance.
  • 七八百載 - Indicates a long period of time, emphasizing the lasting impact of the legendary figure's actions.
  • 功冠三王 - Praises the legendary figure's accomplishments, highlighting his significant contributions.
  • 道德最備 - Highlights the high moral standards of the legendary figure, emphasizing his virtuous character.
  • 是以尊號追及太王 - Indicates the recognition of the legendary figure's contributions after death, reflecting the lasting respect and honor.

Proper Nouns

  • 昭儀 - A high-ranking concubine title in the imperial harem.
  • 泰伯 - A legendary figure in Chinese history, known for his humility and respect for tradition.
  • 王季 - The younger brother of King Tai, who was elevated to a position of power.
  • 太王 - The father of King Tai and King Ji, to whom the posthumous title was bestowed.
  • 趙欽 - A high-ranking official who was stripped of his title and exiled.
  • 成陽侯訴 - Suiqing's nephew, who was also stripped of his title and exiled.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 非常之變 - Refers to extraordinary or unprecedented changes.
  • 非常之謀 - Refers to extraordinary or unprecedented plans.
  • 繼嗣不以時立 - Means 'the heir is not established at the appropriate time.'
  • 萬歲之後 - A euphemism for 'after death.'
  • 權柄之重 - Refers to the significant power or authority.
  • 制於女主 - Means 'controlled by a woman.'
  • 少主幼弱 - Refers to a young and weak ruler.
  • 大臣不使 - Means 'ministers are not allowed to act.'
  • 獨見之明 - Means 'unique insight' or 'independent judgment.'
  • 廢后宮就館之漸 - Refers to the gradual process of removing the queen from the palace.
  • 絕微嗣禍亂之根 - Means 'to cut off the root of potential trouble caused by a weak heir.'
  • 致位陛下 - Means 'to place you in the position of emperor.'
  • 固爭防禍於未然 - Means 'to firmly argue and prevent disasters before they happen.'
  • 阿從以求容媚 - Means 'to follow and flatter to gain favor.'
  • 晏駕之後 - A euphemism for 'after the emperor's death.'
  • 尊號已定 - Means 'the title is already established.'
  • 萬事已訖 - Means 'all matters are settled.'
  • 大德不拘俗 - Means 'great virtue is not bound by conventions.'
  • 大功不合眾 - Means 'great achievements do not conform to the opinions of the masses.'
  • 至思所以萬萬於眾臣 - Means 'the profound thought that surpasses the wisdom of many ministers.'
  • 聖德盛茂 - Refers to 'excellent and abundant virtue.'
  • 符合於皇天 - Means 'in harmony with the will of heaven.'
  • 庸庸斗宵之臣 - Refers to 'ordinary and petty officials.'
  • 褒廣將順君父之美 - Means 'to praise and promote the virtues of the sovereign and father.'
  • 匡救銷滅既往之過 - Means 'to correct and eliminate past mistakes.'
  • 古今通義 - Refers to 'universal principles of ancient and modern times.'
  • 逆負先帝憂國之意 - Means 'to betray the concern of the late emperor for the state.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 孝成皇帝 - Refers to Emperor Cheng of Han, the ruler of the Western Han Dynasty.
  • 陛下 - A respectful term for the emperor.

  • 周公抱负之辅 - Refers to the legendary advisor Zhou Gong, known for his wisdom and loyalty.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 願下有司議 - Formal expression meaning 'I respectfully request that the officials deliberate on this matter.'
  • 即如臣言 - Means 'if it is as I have said,' indicating a conditional statement.
  • 宜宣布天下 - Means 'it should be proclaimed throughout the land,' suggesting a public announcement.
  • 咸曉知先帝聖意所起 - 'Everyone should understand the intention of the late emperor.' '咸' means 'all' or 'everyone,' and '聖意' refers to the emperor's enlightened will.
  • 不然，空使谤议 - Means 'otherwise, it will lead to baseless slander.' '不然' is a negation meaning 'otherwise' or 'if not,' and '谤议' refers to slander or criticism.
  • 上及山陵，下流后世 - Means 'both above to the imperial mausoleum and below to future generations.' '山陵' refers to the imperial tomb, and '下流后世' means 'flowing down to future generations.'
  • 远闻百蛮，近布海内 - Means 'heard far and wide by the barbarians and spread throughout the land.' '百蛮' refers to various non-Han peoples, and '海内' means 'within the seas,' a euphemism for the entire empire.
  • 甚非先帝托后之意也 - Means 'it would be very contrary to the late emperor's intention in entrusting the throne.' '托后' means 'entrusting the throne.'
  • 盖孝子善述父之志，善成人之事 - Means 'a filial son should well express his father's will and complete his father's affairs.' '盖' is a particle used to introduce a general statement, and '善述' means 'well express.'
  • 惟陛下省察 - Means 'I hope the emperor will consider this.' '惟' is a particle used to introduce a wish or plea, and '省察' means 'consider' or 'reflect.'
  • 亦颇得赵太后力 - Means 'also received considerable support from Empress Dowager Zhao.' '颇得' means 'received considerable support.'
  • 遂不竟其事 - Means 'thus did not pursue the matter further.' '不竟' means 'did not pursue to the end.'
  • 傅太后恩赵太后 - Means 'Empress Dowager Fu favored Empress Dowager Zhao.' '恩' means 'favor' or 'grace.'
  • 赵太后亦归心 - Means 'Empress Dowager Zhao also remained loyal.' '归心' means 'remain loyal' or 'be faithful.'
  • 故成帝母及王太后皆怨之 - Means 'thus the mother of Emperor Cheng and Empress Dowager Wang both resented her.' '故' means 'thus' or 'therefore,' and '怨之' means 'resented her.'
  • 哀帝崩 - Means 'Emperor Ai died.' '崩' is a respectful term for the death of an emperor.
  • 王莽白太后 - Means 'Wang Mang reported to Empress Dowager Wang.' '白' means 'report' or 'inform.'
  • 诏有司曰 - Means 'issued an edict to the officials, saying.' '诏' means 'edict' or 'decree,' and '有司' refers to the relevant officials.
  • 前王太后与昭仪 - Refers to 'the former Empress Dowager Wang and Consort Zhao.' '昭仪' is a title for a high-ranking concubine.
  • 俱侍帷幄 - Means 'both served in the imperial court.' '帷幄' refers to the inner chambers of the palace.
  • 姊弟专宠铜寝 - Means 'sister and brother monopolized the favor in the imperial bedchamber.' '铜寝' refers to the imperial bedchamber.
  • 执贼乱之谋 - Means 'engaged in plans to cause disorder.' '贼乱' means 'disorder' or 'chaos.'
  • 残灭继嗣 - Means 'destroyed the line of succession.' '残灭' means 'destroyed,' and '继嗣' means 'line of succession.'
  • 以危宗庙 - Means 'endangered the imperial house.' '宗庙' refers to the ancestral temple, symbolizing the imperial house.
  • 悖天犯祖 - Means 'violated heaven and offended the ancestors.' '悖' means 'violated,' and '犯' means 'offended.'
  • 无为天下母之义 - Means 'lacked the virtue of being a mother to the world.' '天下母' means 'mother to the world,' a term for an empress dowager.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 先帝 - Refers to the late emperor, in this case, Emperor Ai of Han.
  • 山陵 - Refers to the imperial tomb, symbolizing the emperor's resting place.
  • 百蛮 - Refers to various non-Han peoples, often used in a derogatory sense.
  • 海内 - Refers to the entire empire, a euphemism for 'within the seas.'
  • 孝子 - Refers to a filial son, emphasizing the importance of filial piety in Confucian ethics.
  • 成帝母 - Refers to the mother of Emperor Cheng of Han, Empress Dowager Wang Zhengjun.
  • 王太后 - Refers to Empress Dowager Wang Zhengjun, the mother of Emperor Cheng.
  • 哀帝 - Refers to Emperor Ai of Han, who reigned from 7 BC to 1 AD.
  • 傅太后 - Refers to Empress Dowager Fu, the grandmother of Emperor Ai.
  • 赵太后 - Refers to Empress Dowager Zhao, the mother of Emperor Ai.
  • 昭仪 - Refers to Consort Zhao, a high-ranking concubine of Emperor Ai.
  • 铜寝 - Refers to the imperial bedchamber, where the emperor and his concubines resided.
  • 宗庙 - Refers to the ancestral temple, symbolizing the imperial house and the continuity of the dynasty.
  • 天 - Refers to heaven, often personified as a moral force in Chinese thought.
  • 祖 - Refers to the ancestors, whose favor and protection were believed to be crucial for the stability of the dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 先帝 - The late emperor, Emperor Ai of Han.
  • 山陵 - The imperial tomb.
  • 百蛮 - Various non-Han peoples.
  • 海内 - The entire empire.
  • 孝子 - A filial son.
  • 成帝母 - Empress Dowager Wang Zhengjun, the mother of Emperor Cheng.
  • 王太后 - Empress Dowager Wang Zhengjun.
  • 哀帝 - Emperor Ai of Han.
  • 傅太后 - Empress Dowager Fu, the grandmother of Emperor Ai.
  • 赵太后 - Empress Dowager Zhao, the mother of Emperor Ai.
  • 昭仪 - Consort Zhao, a high-ranking concubine of Emperor Ai.
  • 铜寝 - The imperial bedchamber.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 贬皇太后为孝成皇后 - Formal statement of demotion, meaning 'the former empress dowager was demoted to the rank of Empress Consort of Xiao Cheng.'
  • 徙居北宫 - Means 'was moved to live in the Northern Palace,' indicating a change in residence.
  • 朝请希阔 - Literally 'rarely attending court,' used to describe infrequent participation in court rituals.
  • 失妇道，元无养之礼 - Means 'failed in the duties of a wife and lacked the rituals of nurturing,' referring to moral and ritual failings.
  • 而有狼虎之毒 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'possessing the ferocity of wolves and tigers,' emphasizing cruelty.
  • 宗室所怨，海内之仇也 - Means 'hated by the royal clan and an enemy of the people,' highlighting widespread opposition.
  • 尚在小君之位 - Means 'still holding the position of a junior consort,' referring to her status as a lower-ranking empress.
  • 诚非皇天之心 - Means 'truly not in accordance with the will of heaven,' invoking divine disapproval.
  • 小不忍，乱大谋 - A proverb meaning 'small indulgences can disrupt great plans,' emphasizing the need for self-restraint.
  • 恩之所不能已者，义之所割也 - Means 'what cannot be stopped by kindness is what must be cut off by righteousness,' highlighting the conflict between emotion and duty.
  • 废皇后为庶人 - Means 'the empress was deposed and reduced to the status of a commoner.'
  • 就其园 - Means 'she went to her garden,' referring to her place of confinement or execution.
  • 凡立十六年而诛 - Means 'she was in power for sixteen years before being executed,' summarizing her reign and fate.
  • 燕燕尾涎涎 - A metaphorical expression, possibly referring to the fluttering of a bird's tail or a sign of impending doom.
  • 张公子，时相见 - Means 'the Prince Zhang, often seen together,' referring to the frequent meetings with the Prince Zhang.
  • 木门仓琅根 - A metaphorical expression, possibly referring to the sound of metal doors or a symbol of authority.
  • 燕飞来，啄皇孙 - Means 'the swallows fly in and peck at the imperial heirs,' symbolizing harm to the royal lineage.
  • 皇孙死，燕啄矢 - Means 'the imperial heirs die, and the swallows peck at their corpses,' further emphasizing the dire consequences.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 妇道 - Refers to the moral and ritual duties expected of a wife.
  • 狼虎之毒 - A metaphor for extreme cruelty, often used in historical contexts to describe tyrannical behavior.
  • 宗室 - Refers to members of the royal family.
  • 海内 - Refers to the entire realm or country.
  • 小君 - A term for a lower-ranking consort or empress.
  • 皇天 - Refers to heaven or the divine, often invoked in imperial contexts.
  • 张公子 - Refers to the Prince Zhang, a noble figure.
  • 皇孙 - Refers to the imperial heirs or princes.

Proper Nouns

  • 孝成皇后 - The demoted empress.
  • 北宫 - The Northern Palace.
  • 成帝 - Emperor Cheng of Han, the ruler at the time.
  • 张公子 - The Prince Zhang.
  • 木门仓琅根 - A metaphorical expression, possibly from a poem or historical account.
  • 燕飞来，啄皇孙 - A metaphorical expression, possibly from a poem or historical account.
  • 皇孙 - The imperial heirs.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 趙后飛燕 - Refers to Zhao Feiyan, a famous courtesan and later empress during the Han Dynasty.

  • 祖大力 - Means 'her grandfather was strong.'
  • 工理乐器 - Means 'skilled in making musical instruments.'
  • 事江都王协律舍人 - Means 'served as a music official under Prince Jiangdu.'

  • 编习乐声亡章曲 - Means 'compiled music without fixed tunes.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 每泣下凄恻 - Literally 'often weeps with a sad and sorrowful expression,' used to describe the emotional state of the person.
  • 以故白后泣状者，帝辄杀之 - This phrase means 'because of this, when someone reported to the emperor about the queen's weeping, the emperor would kill them.' The phrase '以故' indicates a cause-and-effect relationship.
  • 侍郎宫奴，鲜绔蕴香 - Refers to a palace servant of the rank of Lang (侍郎), and '鲜绔蕴香' means 'wearing fresh, fragrant silk robes.'
  • 恣纵栖息远条馆 - This phrase means 'living freely and comfortably in a distant pavilion.' '恣纵' indicates a state of unrestrained freedom, and '远条馆' refers to a remote pavilion.
  • 元敢言者 - '元' is an archaic form of '无' (wú), meaning 'no' or 'not.' This phrase means 'no one dared to speak up.'
  • 后终无子 - Means 'the queen never had a child.'
  • 后浴五蕴七香汤 - Means 'the queen bathed in a five-ingredient, seven-fragrance bath.' '五蕴' (wǔ yùn) and '七香' (qī xiāng) are specific types of baths with multiple ingredients and fragrances.
  • 踞通香沉水坐，燎降神百蕴香 - '踞通香沉水坐' means 'sitting on a seat made of fragrant wood and incense,' and '燎降神百蕴香' means 'burning a hundred-ingredient incense to summon the gods.'
  • 婕妤浴豆寇汤，傅露华百英粉 - '婕妤浴豆寇汤' means 'the concubine bathed in a bean蔻bath,' and '傅露华百英粉' means 'applied a powder made of a hundred kinds of dew and flowers.'
  • 帝常私语樊曰 - '帝常私语樊曰' means 'the emperor often privately told Fan.' '私语' means 'private conversation,' and '樊' is a proper name, likely referring to a trusted confidant.
  • 后虽有异香，不着婕妤体自香也 - '后虽有异香，不着婕妤体自香也' means 'even though the queen has a unique fragrance, it does not naturally emit from the concubine's body.' '异香' means 'unique fragrance,' and '不着' means 'not emitting.'
  • 江都易王故姬李阳华 - '江都易王故姬李阳华' means 'the former concubine Li Yanghua of the Prince of Jiangdu.' '江都易王' refers to a prince, and '故姬' means 'former concubine.'
  • 其姑为冯大力妻 - '其姑为冯大力妻' means 'her aunt was the wife of Feng Dali.' '其姑' means 'her aunt,' and '冯大力' is a proper name.
  • 阳华老，归冯氏 - '阳华老，归冯氏' means 'Li Yanghua grew old and returned to the Feng family.' '归' means 'return' or 'return to.'
  • 后姊弟母事阳华 - '后姊弟母事阳华' means 'the queen's siblings treated Li Yanghua as a mother.' '母事' means 'treated as a mother.'
  • 善贲饰，常教后九回沉水香泽 - '善贲饰，常教后九回沉水香泽' means 'she was skilled in adornment and often taught the queen a nine-turn incense oil.' '善贲饰' means 'skilled in adornment,' and '九回沉水香泽' refers to a special incense oil.
  • 雄麝脐内息肌丸 - '雄麝脐内息肌丸' means 'a pill made from the testicles of a male musk deer to soothe the skin.' '雄麝脐' means 'testicles of a male musk deer,' and '内息肌丸' means 'a pill to soothe the skin.'
  • 婕妤亦内息肌丸 - '婕妤亦内息肌丸' means 'the concubine also took the pill to soothe the skin.'
  • 常试若为妇者，月事益薄 - '常试若为妇者，月事益薄' means 'she often tested whether the pill would affect menstruation, and it became lighter.' '常试' means 'often tested,' and '月事益薄' means 'menstruation became lighter.'
  • 他日，后言于承光司剂者 - '他日，后言于承光司剂者' means 'one day, the queen spoke to the person in charge of the medicine in the palace.' '承光司剂者' refers to the person responsible for managing the medicines in the palace.
  • 上官妩抚膺曰 - '上官妩抚膺曰' means 'Shangguan Wu, the person in charge, touched her chest and said.' '抚膺' means 'touched her chest,' and '上官妩' is a proper name.
  • 若如是，安能有子乎 - '若如是，安能有子乎' means 'if it is like this, how can she have a child?' '若如是' means 'if it is like this,' and '安能有子乎' means 'how can she have a child?'
  • 教后煮美花涤之 - '教后煮美花涤之' means 'she taught the queen to boil beautiful flowers to cleanse herself.' '美花' means 'beautiful flowers,' and '涤之' means 'to cleanse.'
  • 终不能验 - '终不能验' means 'she could never verify the results.'
  • 真腊夷献万年蛤、不夜珠 - '真腊夷献万年蛤、不夜珠' means 'the people of Champa presented the emperor with a thousand-year-old clam and a pearl that never fades.' '真腊' refers to Champa, '万年蛤' means 'thousand-year-old clam,' and '不夜珠' means 'pearl that never fades.'
  • 光彩皆若月，照人亡妍丑皆美艳 - '光彩皆若月，照人亡妍丑皆美艳' means 'the light and color were like the moon, illuminating everyone and making them all appear beautiful.' '光彩' means 'light and color,' '若月' means 'like the moon,' '照人' means 'illuminating everyone,' and '亡妍丑皆美艳' means 'making everyone appear beautiful.'
  • 帝以蛤赐后，以珠赐婕妤 - '帝以蛤赐后，以珠赐婕妤' means 'the emperor gave the clam to the queen and the pearl to the concubine.'
  • 后以蛤妆五成金霞帐，帐中常若满月 - '后以蛤妆五成金霞帐，帐中常若满月' means 'the queen used the clam to decorate a five-layered golden cloud canopy, and the canopy always looked like a full moon.' '五成金霞帐' means 'five-layered golden cloud canopy,' and '帐中常若满月' means 'the canopy always looked like a full moon.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 婕妤泣怨帝曰 - The imperial concubine wept and complained to the emperor.
  • 非姊赐吾，死不知此器 - If it were not for my sister's gift, I would not know this object even in death.
  • 帝谢之 - The emperor thanked her.
  • 诏益州留三年输，为婕妤作七成锦帐，以沉水香饰 - The emperor decreed that Yizhou should keep the tribute for three years and make a seven-layered brocade canopy for the imperial concubine, adorned with agarwood.
  • 婕妤接帝于大液池，作千人舟，号合宫之舟 - The imperial concubine met the emperor at the Daliyue Pool and built a boat for a thousand people, calling it the 'He Gong Boat.'
  • 池中起为瀛洲，谢高四十丈 - In the middle of the pool, a platform was built as the island of Yingzhou, forty zhang high.
  • 帝御流波文无缝衫，后衣南越所贡云英紫裙，碧琼轻绡广树 - The emperor wore a seamless robe with flowing waves, while the empress wore a purple cloud-embroidered skirt from Nanyue, adorned with green jade and light gauze.
  • 帝以文犀簪击玉瓯，今后所爱侍良冯无方吹笙以倚后歌 - The emperor tapped a jade bowl with a rhinoceros horn hairpin, and the favored servant Feng Wufang played the sheng to accompany the empress's singing.
  • 中流歌酣，风大起，后顺凤扬音，无方长嗡细袅以相属 - In the midst of the song, the wind rose greatly, and the empress, following the wind, raised her voice, while Feng Wufang played the sheng in a long, soft, and delicate manner to accompany her.
  • 仙乎，仙乎，去故而就新，宁忘怀乎 - Oh, divine, oh, divine, leaving the old to embrace the new, how could one forget?
  • 无方舍吹持后履 - Feng Wufang stopped playing and held the empress's shoes.
  • 怅然曼啸，泣数行下 - She sighed deeply, a long, mournful cry, with tears streaming down her face.
  • 帝益愧爱 - The emperor felt even more ashamed and affectionate.
  • 后赐无方千万入后房闼 - The empress granted Feng Wufang a thousand coins to enter her inner chambers.
  • 他日宫姝幸者，或襞裙为绉，号曰留仙裙 - Later, among the favored palace beauties, some would fold their skirts into pleats, calling them 'immortal-retaining skirts.'
