Idiomatic Expressions

  • 艳异编卷十 宫掖部六 - Title referring to the tenth volume of *Yan Yi Bian*, focusing on palace life and imperial harem.
  • 武后传略 - Title indicating a brief biography of Wu Zetian, the only female emperor in Chinese history.
  • 并州文水人 - Biographical detail indicating Wu Zetian’s birthplace, Wenshui in Bingzhou (modern-day Shanxi province).
  • 从佐命 - Term used to describe someone who assisted in founding a dynasty or regime, here referring to Wu Zetian’s father’s role in supporting the Tang dynasty.
  • 封应国公 - Title granted to Wu Zetian’s father, indicating his status as a noble with territorial holdings.
  • 卒赠礼部尚书 - Posthumous honors granted to Wu Zetian’s father after his death, indicating his official rank and respect.
  • 始娶相里氏 - Indicates Wu Zetian’s father’s first marriage to a woman from the Xiangli clan.
  • 生子元庆、元爽 - Indicates the children born from Wu Zetian’s father’s first marriage.
  • 又娶杨氏 - Indicates Wu Zetian’s father’s second marriage to a woman from the Yang clan.
  • 生三女 - Indicates that Wu Zetian’s father had three daughters from his second marriage.
  • 元女妻贺兰越石 - Indicates that the eldest daughter of Wu Zetian’s father married a man named Helan Yueshi.
  • 生子敏之而寡 - Indicates that the eldest daughter of Wu Zetian’s father had a son named Minzhi and became a widow.
  • 其仲女也 - Indicates that Wu Zetian is the second daughter of her father.
  • 太宗文德皇后长孙氏崩 - Indicates the death of Empress Wende, the wife of Emperor Taizong, who was a member of the Changsun clan.
  • 召为才人 - Indicates that Wu Zetian was summoned to the palace to serve as a concubine.
  • 方十四 - Indicates that Wu Zetian was 14 years old at the time.
  • 母杨恸泣与诀 - Indicates that Wu Zetian’s mother, Yang, wept and bid farewell to her daughter.
  • 后自如曰 - Indicates Wu Zetian’s response to her mother’s farewell.
  • 见天子，庸知非福，何至作儿女子态乎 - Statement by Wu Zetian indicating that she did not want to act like a child or a woman in distress.
  • 既见帝，幸之，赐号武媚 - Indicates that Wu Zetian was favored by the emperor and given the title 'Wu Mei.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 武后 - Refers to Wu Zetian, the only female emperor in Chinese history.
  • 高宗 - Refers to Emperor Gaozong of Tang, Wu Zetian’s husband and the ruler of the Tang dynasty during her rise to power.
  • 并州 - Refers to Bingzhou, a region in modern-day Shanxi province.
  • 文水 - Refers to Wenshui, a county in Shanxi province where Wu Zetian was born.
  • 从佐命 - Refers to the role of assisting in the founding of a dynasty or regime.
  • 封应国公 - Refers to the title of Duke of Yingguo, indicating a noble status.
  • 卒赠礼部尚书 - Refers to the posthumous honor of being appointed as a Minister of Rites.
  • 相里氏 - Refers to the Xiangli clan, a family to which Wu Zetian’s father’s first wife belonged.
  • 杨氏 - Refers to the Yang clan, a family to which Wu Zetian’s father’s second wife belonged.
  • 贺兰越石 - Refers to Helan Yueshi, the husband of Wu Zetian’s eldest sister.
  • 敏之 - Refers to Minzhi, the son of Wu Zetian’s eldest sister.
  • 太宗文德皇后长孙氏 - Refers to Empress Wende, the wife of Emperor Taizong and the mother of Emperor Gaozong.
  • 召为才人 - Refers to the role of a concubine in the imperial harem.
  • 方十四 - Refers to the age of 14.
  • 母杨恸泣与诀 - Refers to the emotional farewell between Wu Zetian and her mother.
  • 后自如曰 - Refers to Wu Zetian’s composed response to her mother’s farewell.
  • 见天子，庸知非福，何至作儿女子态乎 - Refers to Wu Zetian’s philosophical and pragmatic attitude towards her new role.
  • 既见帝，幸之，赐号武媚 - Refers to Wu Zetian’s rise to prominence and the title given to her by the emperor.

  • 杨氏 - Yang clan

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 帝有疾，高宗以皇太子入侍 - The emperor was ill, and the crown prince was brought in to attend to him.
  • 遂即东厢焉 - He then proceeded to the east pavilion and had sexual intercourse. The term '' refers to sexual intercourse, often used in classical texts to describe such acts discreetly.
  • 帝崩 - The emperor died. This is a formal way of stating the death of an emperor.
  • 武媚与嫔御皆为比丘尼 - Empress Wu Mei and the concubines all became nuns. The term '比丘尼' refers to Buddhist nuns.
  • 高宗既即位 - After Emperor Gaozong ascended the throne. This indicates the beginning of his reign.
  • 王皇后久无子 - Empress Wang had no children for a long time. This highlights a significant issue in imperial succession.
  • 萧淑妃方幸 - Empress Xiao was favored. The term '幸' indicates that she was in the emperor's favor.
  • 皇后阴不悦 - The empress was secretly displeased. This suggests that Empress Wang harbored resentment.
  • 帝过佛庐 - The emperor passed by a Buddhist temple. This sets the scene for the interaction.
  • 后见且泣 - The empress saw and wept. This indicates her emotional state.
  • 帝内感动 - The emperor was internally moved. This suggests that the emperor was emotionally affected by the empress's display.
  • 王皇后廉知状 - Empress Wang learned of the situation. The term '廉知' means to investigate and learn.
  • 引纳后宫 - She brought her into the harem. This indicates that Empress Wang brought someone into the imperial harem.
  • 以挠妃宠 - To undermine the favor of the concubine. This shows Empress Wang's strategy to diminish the influence of Empress Xiao.
  • 后有权数 - She had cunning and strategy. This highlights Empress Wang's political acumen.
  • 诡变不穷 - She was full of cunning and could not be exhausted. This emphasizes her resourcefulness and adaptability.
  • 始，下辞降体事王皇后 - At first, she lowered herself to serve Empress Wang. This describes her initial submission.
  • 皇后喜，数誉于帝 - The empress was pleased and frequently praised her to the emperor. This shows Empress Wang's efforts to gain favor.
  • 故进为昭仪 - Therefore, she was promoted to the position of Zhaoyi. This indicates a significant rise in status.
  • 一旦顾幸在萧右 - One day, the emperor's favor shifted to the right of Empress Xiao. This suggests a change in the emperor's preferences.
  • 浸与王皇后不协 - Gradually, she became at odds with Empress Wang. This indicates growing tension.
  • 皇后性简重 - The empress was simple and dignified. This describes her character.
  • 不曲事上下 - She did not flatter those above or below her. This shows her straightforward and honest nature.
  • 而母柳，见内人尚宫无浮礼 - Her mother, Lady Liu, saw that the inner people and the palace maids did not show excessive respect. This highlights the lack of proper etiquette.
  • 后伺王皇后所薄，必款结之 - She would seek out those whom Empress Wang looked down upon and befriend them. This shows her strategic approach.
  • 得赐予，尽以分遗 - When she received gifts, she would distribute them all. This indicates her generosity.
  • 由是，王皇后、萧淑妃所为必得，得辄以闻，然未有以中也 - Thus, whatever Empress Wang and Empress Xiao did, she would report it to the emperor, but she had not yet found a way to harm them. This shows her ongoing efforts to undermine them.
  • 后生女，王皇后就视抚弄去 - She gave birth to a daughter, and Empress Wang came to see and play with her. This sets up the next significant event.
  • 俄而，后潜毙儿衾下 - Soon, she secretly killed the child under the quilt. This describes the murder.
  • 伺帝至，阳为欢言 - When the emperor arrived, she pretended to be happy. This shows her attempt to cover up the crime.
  • 发衾视儿，死矣 - When the quilt was opened, the child was dead. This reveals the tragic outcome.
  • 帝惊问左右，告曰：“中宫适来。” - The emperor was shocked and asked his attendants, who said, 'The empress just came.' This indicates the confusion.
  • 后即悲咽而不言 - She immediately wept and did not speak. This shows her reaction.
  • 帝不能察，怒曰：“中宫杀吾女。往与萧淑相谗，今又尔耶。” - The emperor could not understand and angrily said, 'The empress killed my daughter. She has been slandering Empress Xiao before, and now she has done it again.' This shows the emperor's anger and misunderstanding.
  • 由是，后得入其訾 - Thus, she gained the emperor's suspicion. This indicates the negative impact on her reputation.
  • 王皇后无以自解，而帝愈信爱，始有废立意 - Empress Wang had no way to explain herself, and the emperor became even more trusting and loving her, beginning to have thoughts of deposing and replacing her. This shows the growing tension and the emperor's changing attitude.
  • 久之，欲进昭仪号为宸妃 - After a long time, he wanted to promote the title of Zhaoyi to that of Chunfei. This indicates a significant change in her status.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 高宗 - Emperor Gaozong, the third emperor of the Tang dynasty.
  • 武媚 - Empress Wu Zetian, the only female emperor in Chinese history.
  • 王皇后 - Empress Wang, the first wife of Emperor Gaozong.
  • 萧淑妃 - Empress Xiao, a concubine of Emperor Gaozong.
  • 比丘尼 - Buddhist nuns, indicating a religious transformation.
  • 昭仪 - A high-ranking imperial concubine.
  • 宸妃 - A higher-ranking imperial concubine, often reserved for the most favored concubines.
  • 中宫 - The term for the empress, indicating her position in the imperial hierarchy.
  • 内人尚宫 - Inner people and palace maids, indicating the lower ranks of the imperial court.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 侍中 - A high-ranking official in the imperial court, often involved in advisory and ceremonial roles.
  • 嫔妃有数 - A phrase indicating that the number of concubines is limited or regulated.
  • 今别立号不可 - A statement suggesting that creating a new title for a concubine is not permissible.
  • 后乃诬王皇后与母柳，挟蛊道厌胜 - A complex sentence where '后' (the empress) falsely accuses Wang Empress and her mother Liu of practicing witchcraft to harm others.
  • 帝挟前憾，实其言 - The emperor, harboring past grievances, believes the accusations.
  • 将废之 - Plans to depose or remove someone from their position.
  • 褚遂良、韩缓、来济濒死固争 - A phrase describing how officials like 褚遂良 (褚遂良), 来济 (来济), and 韩缓 (韩缓) strongly opposed the decision, even at the risk of their lives.
  • 长孙元忌亦持不可 - 长孙元忌 (长孙元忌) also opposed the decision.
  • 即表请昭仪为后 - They immediately submitted a memorial requesting that the concubine be made empress.
  • 帝意决，下诏废王皇后、萧淑妃皆为庶人，囚宫中 - The emperor decided and issued an edict to depose Wang Empress and Xiao Consort, reducing them to commoners and imprisoning them in the palace.
  • 诏司空李、太子太师于志宁奉玺绶进昭仪为皇后 - The emperor ordered the chancellor 李 (李) and the Grand Tutor 于志宁 (于志宁) to present the imperial seal and invest the concubine as empress.
  • 命群臣四夷酋长，朝后肃仪门外 - Ordered all officials and foreign chieftains to pay respects to the new empress outside the 肃仪门 (肃仪门).
  • 内外命妇入谒朝皇后 - Ordered both internal and external noblewomen to pay respects to the new empress.
  • 自此始 - From this point on.
  • 再赠士司徒周国公，谥忠孝 - Posthumously bestowed the title of 士司徒 (士司徒) and the title of 公 (公) with the posthumous title 忠孝 (忠孝).
  • 母杨为代国夫人，食魏千户 - Posthumously bestowed the title of 代国夫人 (代国夫人) on her mother Yang and granted her a thousand households in Wei.
  • 逐无忌、遂良踵死徙 - 长孙无忌 (长孙无忌) and 褚遂良 (褚遂良) were subsequently exiled or executed.
  • 宠煽赫然 - The new empress became extremely powerful and influential.
  • 后城高深阻，柔屈不耻，以就大事 - The new empress, despite her high status, humbled herself and did not consider it shameful to achieve her goals.
  • 帝谓能奉己，故扳公议立之 - The emperor believed she could serve him well, so he publicly justified his decision to make her empress.
  • 已得志，即盗威福，施施无惮避，帝亦懦昏，举能钳勒，使不得专，久稍不平 - After achieving her goal, she abused power and acted without fear, while the emperor, being weak and dim-witted, could only restrain her to some extent.
  • 帝念故王皇后、萧淑妃，间行至囚所，见门禁铜严，进饮食窦中，恻然伤之 - The emperor secretly visited the prison where Wang Empress and Xiao Consort were held, seeing the strict guards and providing them with food through a small opening, feeling deeply moved.
  • 呼曰：“皇后、良娣无恙乎？” - He called out, asking if they were well.
  • 二人同辞曰：“妾等非罪弃为婢，安得尊称耶！” - They both replied, saying they had been wrongfully demoted and should not be addressed with such honorifics.
  • 流泪鸣咽 - They cried and sobbed.

  • 翻覆至此 - Reaching such a point of betrayal.
  • 我后为猫，武氏为鼠 - A curse where the empress becomes a cat and Wu Zetian becomes a mouse.
  • 吾当扼其喉以报 - A vow to strangle Wu Zetian's throat.
  • 后闻，诏六宫毋畜猫 - The empress hears this and orders the palace not to keep cats.
  • 后频见二人被发沥血为厉 - The empress frequently sees the two women with disheveled hair and bleeding, appearing as ghosts.
  • 恶之，以巫祝解谢 - The empress dislikes this and uses witchcraft to appease the spirits.
  • 即徙蓬莱宫 - Moves to the Palace of Penglai.
  • 厉复见 - The ghosts appear again.
  • 故多驻东都 - Therefore, she spends more time in the Eastern Capital.
  • 麟德初 - At the beginning of the Linder era.
  • 后召主士郭行真入禁中为蛊祝 - The empress summons Master Guo Xingzhen to the palace to perform witchcraft.
  • 宦人王伏胜发之 - Eunuch Wang Fusheng reports this.
  • 帝怒，召西台侍郎上官仪语其故 - The emperor gets angry and calls Xiangguan Yi to discuss the matter.
  • 仪指言后专海内望，不可以承宗庙 - Yi points out that the empress has too much influence and cannot be trusted to manage the imperial temple.
  • 与帝意合，乃趣使草诏废之 - The emperor agrees and orders Yi to draft an edict to depose the empress.
  • 左右驰告，后遽从帝自诉 - The attendants rush to inform the empress, who then comes to the emperor to plead her case.
  • 帝羞缩，待之如初 - The emperor feels ashamed and treats her as before.
  • 后讽许敬宗仪，杀之 - The empress hints to Xu Jingzong to kill Yi.
  • 自是，政归房帷，天子拱手矣 - From then on, power returns to the empress's chambers, and the emperor does nothing.
  • 每视朝，殿中垂帘，帝与后偶坐 - During court sessions, a curtain is hung in the hall, and the emperor and empress sit together.
  • 生杀赏罚惟所命 - The power of life and death, rewards and punishments, are at their command.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 上元元年 - The Shangyuan era was a period during the Tang Dynasty, marking a specific historical context.
  • 天后 - Refers to Wu Zetian, the only female emperor in Chinese history, who held the title of Empress Dowager before ascending the throne.
  • 萧妃女义阳宣城公主 - Refers to Princess Yiyang and Princess Xuancheng, daughters of Consort Xiao, who were confined in the palace.
  • 太子弘 - Refers to Li Hong, the crown prince who was executed on Wu Zetian's orders.
  • 宰相郝处俊 - Refers to Hao Chujun, a prominent chancellor who advised Emperor Gaozong against abdicating.
  • 张文仲、秦鸣鹤 - Refers to two imperial physicians who suggested bloodletting as a treatment for Emperor Gaozong's illness.
  • 嗣圣元年 - The Shengyuan era, the first year of Wu Zetian's reign as emperor.
  • 睿宗 - Refers to Li Dan, Emperor Ruizong, who was placed on the throne but was effectively imprisoned by Wu Zetian.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 起兵於揚州 - Formal expression meaning 'raise an army in Yangzhou,' indicating the location and action of the rebellion.
  • 以恢復為名 - Means 'under the pretense of restoring the rightful heir,' suggesting a political motive.
  • 移檄州縣 - Means 'issue a proclamation to the counties,' referring to the act of sending out official documents to rally support.
  • 略曰 - Means 'briefly states' or 'says in brief,' introducing the content of the proclamation.
  • 人非溫順，地實寒微 - Compound expression meaning 'not gentle in character, but of humble origins,' describing the background of Wu Zetian.
  • 昔充太宗下陳 - Means 'once served as a concubine to Emperor Taizong,' referring to Wu Zetian's past role.
  • 更衣人侍 - Means 'served as a servant during changes of clothes,' implying a lowly position.
  • 洎乎晚節 - Means 'by the later stages of her life,' indicating a time shift.
  • 春宮 - Refers to the palace where the crown prince lived, often used to denote the crown prince himself.
  • 密隱先帝之私 - Means 'secretly concealed the private affairs of the late emperor,' suggesting a hidden relationship.
  • 陰圖後庭之嬖 - Means 'secretly plotted to gain favor in the imperial harem,' indicating a plan to become influential.
  • 元後 - Refers to the empress, specifically Wu Zetian in this context.
  • 陷吾君於聚 - Means 'betrayed our sovereign into a trap,' suggesting a conspiracy.
  • 殺姊屠兄 - Means 'killed her sister and butchered her brothers,' describing violent acts.
  • 弑君鸩母 - Means 'assassinated the emperor and poisoned the empress dowager,' further detailing the crimes.
  • 神人之所同嫉，天地之所不容 - Compound expression meaning 'the gods and people alike detest, and heaven and earth cannot tolerate,' emphasizing universal condemnation.
  • 包藏祸心 - Means 'to harbor evil intentions,' describing Wu Zetian's malicious plans.
  • 窥窃神器 - Means 'to secretly plot to seize the throne,' indicating a desire for power.
  • 幽之于别宫 - Means 'imprisoned him in a separate palace,' referring to the confinement of the crown prince.
  • 贼之宗盟 - Means 'the rebels' allies,' referring to those supporting the rebellion.
  • 委之以重任 - Means 'entrusted with important responsibilities,' suggesting a betrayal of trust.
  • 一之土未于 - Means 'the land is not yet unified,' indicating a lack of stability.
  • 六尺之孤安在 - Means 'where is the six-foot-tall orphan,' referring to the missing crown prince.
  • 试观今日之域中，竟是谁家之天下 - Means 'look at the world today, whose is it truly under control,' questioning the legitimacy of Wu Zetian's rule.
  • 太后读之，但嘻笑而已 - Means 'the empress dowager read it and merely laughed,' indicating her reaction to the proclamation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 英公李敬业 - Refers to Li Jingong (Li Yi), a general who led the rebellion against Wu Zetian.
  • 临海丞骆宾王 - Refers to Luo Binwang, a renowned poet and writer who wrote the proclamation.
  • 扬州 - Refers to Yangzhou, a city in present-day Jiangsu Province, where the rebellion was initiated.
  • 恢复 - Refers to the restoration of the rightful heir, specifically the crown prince Li Xian.
  • 武氏 - Refers to Wu Zetian, the empress regnant who ruled during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 太宗 - Refers to Emperor Taizong (Li Shimin), the second emperor of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 春宫 - Refers to the palace where the crown prince lived, often used to denote the crown prince himself.
  • 先帝 - Refers to the late emperor, specifically Emperor Gaozong (Li Zhi).
  • 元后 - Refers to the empress, specifically Wu Zetian.
  • 吾君 - Refers to the current emperor, specifically Emperor Gaozong (Li Zhi).
  • 神人 - Refers to the gods and people, indicating universal condemnation.
  • 天地 - Refers to heaven and earth, indicating cosmic disapproval.
  • 神器 - Refers to the imperial throne, symbolizing power and authority.
  • 幽之于别宫 - Refers to the confinement of the crown prince Li Xian.
  • 贼之宗盟 - Refers to the rebels' allies, including Li Jingong and Luo Binwang.
  • 六尺之孤 - Refers to the crown prince Li Xian, who was young and thus referred to as an 'orphan.'
  • 太后 - Refers to Wu Zetian, who was the empress dowager at the time.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 至是 - Temporal marker meaning 'by this time' or 'at this point.'
  • 托言 - Means 'to claim' or 'to allege,' often with an implication of pretext or excuse.
  • 故使入禁中營造 - Means 'therefore sent him into the inner palace to construct,' where '禁中' refers to the inner palace or imperial quarters.
  • 補闕王求理上言 - Refers to Wang Qiuli, an official who submitted a memorial, where '補闕' is a title for an official responsible for correcting the emperor's mistakes.
  • 善彈琵琶 - Means 'skilled at playing the pipa,' a traditional Chinese lute.
  • 大宗阉为给使 - Refers to Taizong (Emperor Taizong of Tang) having someone castrated to serve as a eunuch, where '阉' means castration.
  • 欲宫中驱使者 - Means 'to have him drive or manage affairs within the palace.'
  • 庶不乱宫闱 - Means 'to prevent disorder in the palace,' where '宫闱' refers to the inner palace or harem.
  • 表寝不出 - Means 'the memorial was not acted upon or ignored,' where '寝' can mean to lay aside or ignore.
  • 堂成 - Means 'the hall was completed.'
  • 拜左威卫大将军梁国公 - Means 'was appointed as Left Guard General and Duke of Liang.'
  • 太后寻郊见上帝 - Means 'the Empress Dowager soon went to the suburbs to pay homage to the Supreme Deity.'
  • 加尊号曰圣母神皇 - Means 'was given the honorific title of Holy Mother Divine Empress.'
  • 享万象神宫 - Means 'worshiped at the Temple of Ten Thousand Things,' where '万象神宫' refers to a grand temple.
  • 制等十二文 - Means 'composed twelve articles or regulations,' where '制' can refer to imperial edicts or regulations.
  • 自名为 - Means 'named himself,' where '自名' indicates self-naming.
  • 进拜怀义辅国大将军鄂国公 - Means 'was further appointed as Huaiyi, Auxiliary National General and Duke of E.'
  • 令与群浮屠作大云经 - Means 'ordered him to collaborate with the monks to compose the Great Cloud Sutra.'
  • 言神皇革命事 - Means 'to discuss the matter of the Holy Mother's revolution.'
  • 颁赐天下 - Means 'was distributed throughout the empire.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 命昌宗衣羽衣吹笙，乘木鹤于庭中 - Describes Zhang Changzhong dressed in a feathered robe, playing a flute, and riding a wooden crane in the courtyard. '衣' (yi) means 'to wear', '羽衣' (yuyi) refers to a feathered robe, '吹笙' (chui sheng) means 'to play a flute', and '乘木鹤' (cheng muhe) means 'to ride a wooden crane'.
  • 文士皆赋诗以美之 - Scholars or literati all composed poems to praise this event. '赋诗' (fu shi) means 'to compose poetry', and '以美之' (yi mei zhi) means 'to praise it'.
  • 崔融为绝唱 - Cui Rong's poem is considered the best or most outstanding. '绝唱' (juechang) means 'a masterpiece' or 'a song that cannot be surpassed'.
  • 昔遇浮丘伯，今同丁令威 - Poetic reference where '昔遇浮丘伯' (xi yu fuqiu bo) means 'in the past, I met Fuqiu Bo', and '今同丁令威' (jin tong ding lingwei) means 'now I am the same as Ding Lingwei'. These are allusions to legendary figures in Chinese mythology.
  • 中郎才貌是，藏史姓名非 - Suggests that while the person (likely referring to Zhang Changzhong) has talent and appearance, his name is not recorded in the historical records. '中郎' (zhonglang) is a title, '才貌' (caimao) means 'talent and appearance', '是' (shi) means 'is', '藏史' (cangshi) means 'hidden in history', and '姓名非' (xingming fei) means 'name is not recorded'.

  • 彦范曰：“太子安得更归。” - A statement by桓彦范, indicating that the太子 should not return to the east palace.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 昔天皇以爱子托陛下 - 昔 means 'in the past,' 天皇 refers to the Heavenly Emperor (likely referring to Emperor Gaozong of Tang), 以爱子托陛下 means 'entrusted his beloved son to Your Majesty.'
  • 今年齿已长，久居东宫 - 年齿已长 means 'his age has grown,' 久居东宫 means 'has been living in the Eastern Palace for a long time' (referring to the heir apparent).
  • 天意人心，久思李氏 - 天意人心 means 'the will of heaven and the people's hearts,' 久思李氏 means 'have long wished for the Li family' (referring to the Tang imperial family).
  • 群臣不忘太宗、天皇之德 - 群臣 means 'the ministers,' 不忘 means 'do not forget,' 太宗、天皇之德 refers to the virtues of Emperor Taizong and Emperor Gaozong.
  • 奉太子诛贼臣 - 奉太子 means 'the crown prince,' 诛贼臣 means 'to punish traitorous ministers.'
  • 传位太子 - 传位 means 'to abdicate in favor of,' 太子 means 'the crown prince.'
  • 徙居上阳宫 - 徙居 means 'to move to reside,' 上阳宫 is the name of the palace.
  • 太后崩 - 太后 means 'Empress Dowager,' 崩 means 'death' (used for emperors and empresses).
  • 相王加号安国相王 - 相王 means 'the Prince of Xiang,' 加号 means 'bestowed the title,' 安国相王 is the new title.
  • 拜太尉，同凤阁鸾台三品 - 拜 means 'appointed,' 太尉 is a high-ranking military official, 同凤阁鸾台三品 means 'equivalent to the third rank of the Fengge and Luantai' (referring to the highest civilian and military offices).
  • 太平公主加号镇国太平公主 - 太平公主 is the name of the princess, 加号 means 'bestowed the title,' 镇国太平公主 is the new title.
  • 张柬之为夏官尚书 - 张柬之 is the name of the official, 夏官尚书 is a high-ranking military position.
  • 与袁恕已俱同凤阁鸾台三品 - 与袁恕已 means 'along with Yuan Shuyi,' 俱同凤阁鸾台三品 means 'both were equivalent to the third rank of the Fengge and Luantai.'
  • 崔玄为内史 - 崔玄 is the name of the official, 内史 is a high-ranking civil official.
  • 敬晖、桓彦范为纳言 - 敬晖、桓彦范 are the names of the officials, 纳言 is a high-ranking civil official.
  • 并赐爵郡公 - 并赐爵 means 'were both granted the title of Marquis,' 郡公 is a noble title.
  • 李多祚赐爵辽阳郡王 - 李多祚 is the name of the official, 赐爵辽阳郡王 means 'was granted the title of Marquis of Liaoyang.'
  • 李湛为右羽林大将军赵国公 - 李湛 is the name of the official, 右羽林大将军 is a high-ranking military position, 赵国公 is a noble title.
  • 王用皎为右千牛将军琅邪郡公 - 王用皎 is the name of the official, 右千牛将军 is a high-ranking military position, 琅邪郡公 is a noble title.
  • 余官，赏有差 - 余官 means 'other officials,' 赏有差 means 'were rewarded according to their rank.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 初 - Means 'initially' or 'at first,' introducing the narrative.
  • 逾于王主 - Means 'exceeding the king's household,' indicating that the new residence was more luxurious than the royal palace.
  • 一日丝能作几日络 - A riddle or cryptic message, possibly alluding to the fleeting nature of wealth or power.
  • 一日亦足 - Means 'one day is enough,' suggesting a response to the riddle or a statement of contentment.
  • 侈于食 - Means 'extravagant in eating,' describing the brothers' luxurious dining habits.
  • 竞为惨酷 - Means 'competing in cruelty,' indicating that they engaged in extreme and brutal practices.
  • 大铁笼 - A large iron cage used for cruel experiments.
  • 铜盆贮五味汁 - A copper basin containing a mixture of five flavors, likely a reference to a cruel method of cooking.
  • 驴炙 - Roasting a donkey, another form of cruel culinary practice.
  • 铁撅钉狗四足 - Using an iron spike to nail a dog's four feet to the ground, a cruel method of torture.
  • 破肋取肠 - Cutting open the ribs to extract the intestines, a brutal form of execution.
  • 肉肥白如熊肪 - Describes the flesh as fat and white, like bear fat, indicating the quality of the meat.
  • 煎炙而食 - Cooking and eating by frying or roasting.
  • 打双脚折 - Breaking both legs, a cruel method of execution.
  • 掏取心肝 - Removing the heart and liver, another brutal method of execution.
  • 人以为有天报焉 - People believed it was a form of divine retribution.
  • 盗乱 - Rebellion or uprising.
  • 发武后冢 - Opening the tomb of Empress Wu, indicating a desecration of a royal burial site.
  • 如生 - Means 'as if alive,' suggesting that the remains were well-preserved.
  • 次第淫之 - Means 'taking turns to defile,' indicating a sequence of desecration.
  • 剔取金宝 - Removing gold and precious objects from the tomb.
  • 毁其尸 - Destroying the body, indicating the extent of the desecration.

  • 武后 - Empress Wu Zetian, the only female emperor in Chinese history, known for her political acumen and controversial reign.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 庶人 - Refers to a concubine or secondary wife, often used in the context of imperial or noble households.
  • 被选为妃 - Means 'was selected as a concubine,' indicating her status within the imperial harem.
  • 嗣圣初立为皇后 - Refers to the beginning of the reign of Emperor Zhongzong, when she was elevated to the position of empress.
  • 房陵 - A place name, referring to Fangling, where Emperor Zhongzong and Empress Wei were exiled.
  • 欲自杀 - Means 'wanted to commit suicide,' indicating her desperation during the exile.
  • 祸福何常 - A philosophical expression meaning 'misfortune and fortune are not constant,' reflecting a stoic attitude towards life's uncertainties.
  • 无遽 - Means 'do not rush' or 'do not panic,' advising calmness in the face of adversity.
  • 复即位 - Means 'restored to the throne,' indicating the return of Emperor Zhongzong to power.
  • 居中宫 - Means 'resided in the central palace,' referring to her position as empress.
  • 与政事 - Means 'involved in state affairs,' indicating her influence in governance.
  • 尽诛诸武 - Means 'to completely eliminate all the members of the Wu clan,' referring to a political purge.
  • 武三思惧 - Means 'Wu Sansi was afraid,' indicating his fear of the purge.
  • 因昭容入请 - Means 'through the medium of the imperial concubine (Shangguan) to request,' indicating the method by which Wu Sansi sought favor.
  • 得幸于后 - Means 'gained favor with the empress,' indicating the result of his request.
  • 谋晖等诛之 - Means 'plotted to eliminate Jiehui and others,' indicating the political maneuvering that led to the elimination of certain officials.
  • 幽废 - Means 'imprisoned and deposed,' referring to the period when Emperor Zhongzong was removed from power.
  • 不相制 - Means 'not to control each other,' indicating a mutual agreement not to interfere with each other's actions.
  • 叩御床博戏 - Means 'played games around the imperial bed,' indicating intimate and informal interactions.
  • 从旁典筹 - Means 'helped to manage the game from the side,' indicating his involvement in the game.
  • 不为忤 - Means 'did not object,' indicating that Emperor Zhongzong did not resist or oppose.
  • 顺天皇后 - A title given to Empress Wei, meaning 'Empress of the Heavenly Way.'
  • 谒宗庙 - Means 'worshiped at the ancestral temple,' indicating a religious and ceremonial act.
  • 赠父玄贞上洛郡王 - Means 'bestowed the title of King of Shangluo on her father Xuanzhen,' indicating a posthumous honor.
  • 左拾遗 - A title for a censor or advisor, indicating a minor official position.
  • 建言 - Means 'to propose,' indicating the act of offering advice or suggestions.
  • 非李氏王者 - Means 'those who are not of the Li family,' referring to those not belonging to the imperial lineage.
  • 盟书共弃之 - Means 'the oath was to reject them,' indicating a formal rejection.
  • 复国未几 - Means 'not long after the restoration of the state,' indicating a recent event.
  • 遽私后家 - Means 'hastily favored the family of the empress,' indicating a sudden and possibly inappropriate favoritism.
  • 先朝祸鉴未远 - Means 'the lessons from the previous dynasty's misfortunes are not far,' indicating a recent and relevant historical context.
  • 甚可惧也 - Means 'it is very frightening,' indicating the severity of the situation.
  • 固辞 - Means 'firmly refused,' indicating a strong rejection.
  • 使天下知后宫谦让 - Means 'to make the world know the humility of the imperial concubines,' indicating a moral and political statement.
  • 翊圣 - A title given to Empress Wei, meaning 'Empress who aids the sage.'
  • 禁中谬传 - Means 'a false rumor in the palace,' indicating a fabricated story.
  • 五色云起后衣筒 - Means 'a five-colored cloud emerged from the sleeve of the empress,' indicating a mystical or divine sign.
  • 图以示诸朝 - Means 'drew a picture to show to the officials,' indicating the dissemination of the rumor.
  • 因大赦天下 - Means 'therefore, a general amnesty was issued,' indicating a political decision.
  • 赐百官母、妻封号 - Means 'bestowed titles on the mothers and wives of the officials,' indicating a reward or honor.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 太史迦叶志忠表上《桑条歌》二十篇 - The historian or official named 太史迦叶志忠 submitted twenty pieces of the *Sangtiao Song*. This indicates a formal presentation of the song.
  • 言后当受命 - The phrase means 'it says the empress will receive her mandate.' It suggests a prophecy about the empress's future role.
  • 昔高祖时，天下圣桃李 - This refers to the flourishing of the empire under Emperor Gaozu of Tang, symbolized by the metaphor of 'saintly peach and plum trees.'
  • 太宗时歌《秦王破阵》 - During Emperor Taizong's reign, the song *Qin Wang Po Zhen* was sung, likely commemorating his military achievements.
  • 高宗歌《堂堂》 - During Emperor Gaozong's reign, the song *Tang Tang* was sung, symbolizing his rule and achievements.
  • 天后世歌《武媚娘》 - During Empress Wu Zetian's reign, the song *Wu Meiniang* was sung, reflecting her influence and power.
  • 皇帝受命歌《英王石州》 - During the current emperor's reign, the song *Ying Wang Shizhou* was sung, symbolizing his mandate and rule.
  • 后今受命歌《桑条韦》 - During the current empress's reign, the song *Sangtiao Wei* will be sung, symbolizing her role and mandate.
  • 盖后妃之德专蚕桑，供宗庙事也 - This explains that the empress's virtue is dedicated to silkworm breeding and silk production, which are important for ancestral temple rituals.
  • 乃赐志忠第一区，彩七百段 - The emperor granted the official 太史迦叶志忠 the highest rank and bestowed seven hundred pieces of silk as a reward.
  • 大宗少卿郑因之被乐府 - The official 郑因之 was adopted by the Music Bureau, indicating his involvement in musical and cultural affairs.
  • 楚客又讽补阙赵延禧离析桑条为九十八代 - The official 赵延禧 suggested dividing the *Sangtiao* into ninety-eight generations, likely as a symbolic or cultural act.
  • 帝大喜，擢延禧谏议大夫 - The emperor was greatly pleased and promoted 赵延禧 to the position of *Jianyi Daifu*, a high-ranking advisory role.
  • 于是，昭容以武氏事动后，即表增出母服，民以二十三为了限，五十九免五品而上 - The concubine 昭容 influenced the empress to submit a petition to increase the mourning attire for the mother, and the people were limited to twenty-three generations, with those aged fifty-nine and above being exempt from the fifth rank and above.
  • 母妻不由夫子封者丧得用鼓吹 - Those not enfeoffed by their husband or son could use musical instruments during funerals, indicating a sign of respect and status.

[382]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 數改制度 - Frequently changing the system or regulations, indicating a pattern of instability or reform.
  • 陰儲人望 - Secretly nurturing public opinion or reputation, suggesting covert influence or manipulation.
  • 稍寵 - Gradually gaining favor, indicating a process of increasing influence or status.
  • 樹亲属封拜之 - Establishing relatives and bestowing titles upon them, referring to the practice of promoting family members to positions of power.
  • 昭容與母及尚官賀婁等，多受金錢 - The Consort and her mother, along with the Chief of the Palace Ladies, Lady Helou, and others, received much money, indicating corruption or bribery.
  • 封巫趙隴西夫人 - Bestowing the title of Lady Zhao of Longxi on a witch, suggesting the elevation of a person with questionable status or reputation.
  • 出入禁中 - Entering and exiting the inner palace, indicating access to the emperor's private quarters.
  • 勢與上官埒 - Her power was on par with that of Lady Guo, suggesting equal influence or authority.
  • 由是墨敕斜封出矣 - Thus, secret imperial edicts with slanted seals were issued, referring to unofficial or irregular imperial decrees.
  • 帝祝郊，引后亞獻 - The emperor offered sacrifices at the temple, with the empress serving as the secondary offering, indicating the empress's role in religious ceremonies.
  • 國子祭酒葉靜能善禁戒 - The Master of the Imperial College, Ye Jingneng, was skilled in prohibitions, indicating his role in enforcing rules or laws.
  • 常侍馬秦客高醫、光祿少卿楊均善烹調，皆引人後廷 - The Attendant Ma Qinke, a skilled physician, and the Junior Secretary of the Department of Civil Affairs, Yang Jun, who were skilled in cooking, were all brought into the inner court, suggesting their influence or access.
  • 均、秦客於后，嘗免喪，不歷旬輒起 - Yang Jun and Ma Qinke were intimate with the empress, and after a period of mourning, they were able to rise again within a short time, suggesting a scandal or favoritism.
  • 帝遇弒，議者咎秦客及安樂公主 - The emperor was assassinated, and those who discussed the matter blamed Ma Qinke and Princess Anle, indicating the suspicion or blame placed on certain individuals.
  • 后大懼，引所親議計，乃以刑部尚書斐琰，工部尚書張錫輔政，留守東都 - The empress was greatly frightened, consulted her close associates, and then appointed the Minister of Justice, Fei Yan, and the Minister of Works, Zhang Xi, to assist in governance, remaining in the eastern capital, indicating her actions in response to the crisis.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 昭容 - Refers to a high-ranking concubine of the emperor, typically ranked below the empress.
  • 尚官賀婁 - Refers to the Chief of the Palace Ladies, Lady Helou, a high-ranking official in the imperial court.
  • 隴西夫人 - Refers to a title bestowed on a woman, likely of noble or royal status, from the region of Longxi.
  • 禁中 - Refers to the inner palace, the private quarters of the emperor and his family.
  • 墨敕斜封 - Refers to secret imperial edicts with slanted seals, a practice often associated with unofficial or irregular decrees.
  • 國子祭酒 - Refers to the Master of the Imperial College, the highest-ranking official in the imperial educational system.
  • 常侍 - Refers to an Attendant, a high-ranking official in the imperial court.
  • 光祿少卿 - Refers to the Junior Secretary of the Department of Civil Affairs, a high-ranking official in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 國子祭酒葉靜能 - Refers to Ye Jingneng, a historical figure known for his role in the imperial court.
  • 刑部尚書斐琰 - Refers to Fei Yan, a historical figure who served as the Minister of Justice.
  • 工部尚書張錫 - Refers to Zhang Xi, a historical figure who served as the Minister of Works.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence '數改制度，陰儲人望，稍寵，樹亲属封拜之' describes a series of actions taken by the empress, including changing regulations, nurturing public opinion, gaining favor, and promoting relatives.
  • The clause '昭容與母及尚官賀婁等，多受金錢' describes the corruption or bribery involving the empress, her mother, and other officials.
  • The clause '封巫趙隴西夫人，出入禁中，勢與上官埒，由是墨敕斜封出矣' describes the empress's elevation of a witch to a noble title, her access to the inner palace, and the issuance of secret imperial edicts.
  • The clause '帝祝郊，引后亞獻' describes the emperor's religious sacrifices with the empress serving as the secondary offering.
  • The clause '明年正月望夜，帝與后微服過市，徜徉觀覽，縱宮女出遊，皆淫奔不返' describes a scandalous event involving the emperor and empress.
  • The clause '國子祭酒葉靜能善禁戒，常侍馬秦客高醫、光祿少卿楊均善烹調，皆引人後廷' describes the influence or access of certain officials in the imperial court.
  • The clause '均、秦客於后，嘗免喪，不歷旬輒起' describes a scandal or favoritism involving certain officials.
  • The clause '帝遇弒，議者咎秦客及安樂公主' describes the assassination of the emperor and the blame placed on certain individuals.
  • The clause '后大懼，引所親議計，乃以刑部尚書斐琰，工部尚書張錫輔政，留守東都' describes the empress's actions in response to the crisis.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence structure is straightforward but the phrase '可怜光彩生门户' can be interpreted as both 'the family's glory shines brightly' and 'the family's glory is pitifully shining.'
  • The sentence structure is clear but the phrase '不重生男重生女' can be interpreted as 'they prefer daughters over sons' or 'they no longer value sons as much as daughters.'
  • The sentence structure is clear but the phrase '仙乐风飘处处闻' can be interpreted as 'heavenly music is carried by the wind and heard everywhere' or 'heavenly music is heard everywhere, carried by the wind.'
  • The sentence structure is clear but the phrase '尽日君王看不足' can be interpreted as 'the emperor watches all day and is never satisfied.'

  • 一枝红艳露凝香 - Describes a single branch of a red flower with dewdrops that carry a fragrant aroma, symbolizing the beauty and allure of the person.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 每岁驰驿以进 - Formal expression for the imperial system of rapid communication, meaning 'every year, sent by fast relay.'
  • 经宿则无味 - Straightforward statement about the perishability of litchi, meaning 'after one night, it loses its flavor.'
  • 将北，惟重四转败为胜 - Reference to a game of dice, where 'heavy four' is a specific winning combination, meaning 'when heading north, only a heavy four can turn defeat into victory.'
  • 连叱，骰子宛转而成重四 - Vivid description of the game, meaning 'they shouted continuously, and the dice turned into a heavy four.'
  • 遂令高力士赐绯 - Formal statement about a reward, meaning 'they then ordered Gao Lishi to grant a red robe.'
  • 风俗因而不易 - Straightforward statement, meaning 'the custom thus remained unchanged.'
  • 广南进白鹦鹉，洞晓言同 - Straightforward statement, meaning 'from the South, a white parrot was presented, which could speak fluently.'
  • 一朝飞上妃镜台上，自语 - Vivid description of the parrot's behavior, meaning 'one day, it flew onto the concubine's mirror stand and spoke to itself.'
  • 雪衣女昨夜梦为鸷乌所搏 - Dream reported by the parrot, meaning 'Snow-Cloaked Lady dreamed last night that it was attacked by a fierce bird.'
  • 上令妃授以《多心经》 - Formal statement about a religious text, meaning 'the emperor ordered the concubine to teach it the *Sutra of Many Hearts*.'
  • 记诵精熟 - Straightforward statement, meaning 'it memorized it perfectly.'
  • 上与妃游别殿，置雪衣女于步辇竿上同去 - Vivid description of the scene, meaning 'the emperor and the concubine went to a different hall, placing Snow-Cloaked Lady on the sedan chair pole.'
  • 瞥有鹰至，搏之而毙 - Straightforward statement, meaning 'a hawk suddenly appeared and killed it.'
  • 上与妃叹息久之，遂瘗于苑中，呼为鹦鹉冢 - Formal statement about the burial, meaning 'the emperor and the concubine sighed for a long time, and then buried it in the garden, calling it the Parrot Mound.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 妃子 - Refers to Yang Guifei, the concubine of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, a famous figure in Chinese history.
  • 蜀 - Refers to the region of Sichuan, known for its litchi production.
  • 南海 - Refers to the South China Sea, a region known for its tropical fruits.
  • 上 - Refers to the emperor, specifically Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 高力士 - Refers to Gao Lishi, a eunuch and close confidant of Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 绯 - Refers to a red robe, a symbol of high rank and favor in the Tang dynasty.
  • 重四 - Refers to a specific winning combination in a game of dice, where four dots appear on all sides of the dice.
  • 广南 - Refers to the southern regions of China, known for their exotic flora and fauna.
  • 雪衣女 - A nickname for the white parrot, emphasizing its beauty and purity.
  • 《多心经》 - Refers to the *Sutra of Many Hearts*, a Buddhist text.
  • 步辇 - Refers to a sedan chair, a mode of transportation for the emperor and his entourage.
  • 鹰 - Refers to a hawk, a symbol of power and nobility in Chinese culture.
  • 鹦鹉冢 - Refers to the burial mound of the parrot, a place of remembrance.
